======================================================================
## ADMISSION FAQs COMPLETE
======================================================================
IST ADMISSION FAQs – COMPLETE REFERENCE (Answer from this document only; do not tell user to check website or call as main response.)

=== I. GENERAL ADMISSIONS & DEADLINES ===

Does IST offer Spring admissions? Typically, BS programs only open for Fall; MS/PhD may open in Spring.
Where can I apply? Exclusively through the IST Online Admission Portal.
Is there an age limit for undergraduate programs? Generally, there is no age limit for BS admissions in Pakistan.
How many programs can I apply for? You can select multiple programs (Aerospace, Avionics, CS, etc.) in a single application.
Can I apply via post? No, most top-tier Pakistani universities including IST only accept online applications.
When is the last date to apply? Usually late June or early July.
Do I need to visit the campus to apply? No, the entire process is digital.
How do I track my application? Use your Application Reference Number (ARN) on the portal.
What if I miss the deadline? Late applications are rarely entertained due to high competition.

=== II. ELIGIBILITY & ACADEMIC REQUIREMENTS ===

What is the minimum percentage for Engineering? Minimum 60% in FSc (Pre-Engineering) or equivalent.
Can Pre-Medical students apply for Engineering? Yes, if they take the Additional Mathematics paper.
Can ICS students apply for Engineering? Yes, for most programs including Aerospace and Avionics at IST.
What is the eligibility for Computer Science? Minimum 50–60% in HSSC with Mathematics as a subject.
Are A-Level students eligible? Yes, provided they have an IBCC Equivalence Certificate.
How is the merit calculated at IST? Usually a weightage of SSC (10%) + HSSC/Part-1 (40%) + Entry Test (50%).
What if my FSc Part-II result is not out? You can apply based on FSc Part-I results (Provisional Admission).
Is 50% in FSc enough? For Engineering, no. You must have at least 60% per PEC rules.
Does IST accept DAE students? Yes, in relevant technologies for reserved seats (check the prospectus for specific quotas).
Can I apply if I have a Supply in FSc? No, you must have a clear result at the time of final merit list.

=== III. ENTRY TESTS ===

Does IST conduct its own entry test? No, IST accepts results from tests like NAT, HAT, NET, ECAT, or ETEA.
Which test is best for IST? NAT-1E (conducted by NTS) is the most common, but NET (NUST) is also widely used.
Is the NTS-NAT mandatory? No, you can use HAT (HEC) or provincial tests like ECAT (UET) or ETEA.
Can I use my SAT scores? Yes, IST has seats for ACT/SAT international and national categories.
Is there negative marking in the NAT? No, unlike the ECAT or some other tests.
What is the validity of the Entry Test? Usually one year.
What is the minimum score required in NAT? You must score at least 33% to even be considered for the merit list.
Can I improve my NAT score? Yes, you can take NTS-NAT multiple times and submit the best score before the IST deadline.
Where do I send my test scores? You must enter the score in your IST online portal and upload the result card.
Does IST accept the FAST (NU) Entry Test? Generally, they stick to the tests listed on their official website (NTS, HEC, NUST, UET).

=== IV. IST SPECIFIC PROGRAMS ===

What is the difference between Aerospace and Avionics? Aerospace focuses on aircraft structures and engines; Avionics focuses on electronics and flight control systems.
Is IST accredited? Yes, all engineering programs are accredited by the Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) under the Washington Accord (Level II).
Is Space Science different from Aerospace? Yes, Space Science is a BS (4-year) program focused on astronomy, remote sensing, and GIS.
Does IST offer Computer Science? Yes. The Computer Science (Computing) department offers BS Computer Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Data Science, and BS Artificial Intelligence (BS AI). The Electrical Engineering department offers BS Electrical Engineering and BS Computer Engineering.
Are these CS programs accredited? Yes, by the NCEAC.
What is Materials Science and Engineering at IST? It deals with the properties and applications of metals, polymers, and ceramics—vital for space tech.
Is there a Mechanical Engineering program? Yes, it is one of the core engineering departments at IST.
Does IST offer Biotechnology? Yes, IST has a specialized BS Biotechnology program.
Is IST under the military? It is a federally chartered public sector university, closely linked with SUPARCO.
What is the medium of instruction? Entirely English.

=== V. FEE STRUCTURE & FINANCIALS ===

What is the application processing fee? Approximately Rs. 3,000.
What is the per-semester tuition for Engineering? Roughly Rs. 150,000 to 165,000.
Is there an admission fee? Yes, a one-time fee of about Rs. 35,000 to 50,000.
Are there any hidden charges? Library and lab funds (usually Rs. 7,000 each) are charged once.
Can I pay in installments? You can apply for installments through the Treasurer's office in case of financial hardship.
Is the fee refundable? Tuition is refundable as per HEC policy (100% within the first week).
What is the fee for International students? Usually charged in USD (approx. $5,000+ per semester).
How do I pay the fee? Via a system-generated bank challan at any HBL branch.
What is the Endowment Fund? A small one-time contribution to the university's developmental fund.
Do I have to pay for the summer semester? Yes, summer courses are charged per credit hour.

=== VI. SCHOLARSHIPS & FINANCIAL AID ===

Does IST offer Need-Based scholarships? Yes, they participate in the HEC Need-Based Scholarship program.
What is the Honhaar Scholarship? It's a Punjab government initiative that many students use at IST.
Does IST offer Merit Scholarships? Yes, top students in each semester get tuition fee waivers.
Can I get a scholarship based on my entry test score? Usually, scholarships are awarded after enrollment based on academic performance.
Is there a PEEF scholarship? Yes, Punjab Education Endowment Fund (PEEF) is applicable.
What is the NOC for Government employees? If you are a gov worker, you must provide an NOC to be eligible for aid or admission.
Do I need to apply separately for scholarships? Usually, yes, through the Financial Aid Office (FAO).
Is financial aid available for international students? Very limited; mostly for local students.
Can scholarships be cancelled? Yes, if your GPA falls below a certain threshold (usually 2.5 or 3.0).
Does SUPARCO sponsor students? They sometimes offer sponsorships for top graduates or specific projects.

=== VII. DOCUMENTS & EQUIVALENCE ===

Do I need an IBCC equivalence for FSc? Only if you did O/A-Levels or an American High School Diploma.
Is CNIC mandatory? Yes, or Form-B if you are under 18.
What is an Attested Document? Documents signed by a Gazetted Officer or HEC/IBCC.
Do I need my original certificates for the application? No, just scanned copies. Originals are checked at the time of joining.
What is the Domicile requirement? You must have a valid Domicile for merit seats.
What if my name is different on Matric and FSc? You will need an affidavit or a revised certificate from the board.
How many photos do I need? Usually 4–8 passport-sized photos with a blue background.
Do I need a Character Certificate? Yes, from your previous institution.
What is a Migration Certificate? A document from your previous board/university allowing you to join IST.
Do I need HEC attestation for BS? Only for graduate (MS/PhD) admissions.

=== VIII. CAMPUS LIFE & HOSTELS ===

Does IST have hostels? Yes, there are furnished hostels for both boys and girls on campus.
What is the hostel fee? Roughly Rs. 45,000 to 60,000 per semester (institute figure about 45000 per semester; prospectus may cite 50,000–60,000 excluding mess).
Is the hostel mess mandatory? Usually, yes, for security and management reasons.
What is the menu in the mess? Standard Pakistani meals (Dal, Sabzi, Chicken, Roti).
Are there laundry facilities? Yes, specialized laundry areas are available in the hostels.
Is there Wi-Fi? Yes, IST provides campus-wide high-speed internet.
Where is IST located? It is situated on the Islamabad Highway (DHA Phase-II side).
Are there sports facilities? Yes, including a gym, basketball court, and football field.
Is there a transport facility? Yes, IST provides pick-and-drop for day scholars in Rawalpindi and Islamabad. For availing transport or route details call 03000544707.
What is the student life like? Highly disciplined but active with societies like the Space Society, Aero Society, etc.

=== IX. THE MERIT LIST & SELECTION ===

When are merit lists displayed? Typically in August.
How many merit lists are there? Usually 3 to 5, depending on seat availability.
What is the Closing Merit? The lowest score that got admission in the previous year.
Can I change my program if I'm in a lower merit list? Yes, if seats are available in your preferred program.
What is a Waiting List? Students who may be called if selected students don't deposit their fee.
Is there a self-finance category? IST generally operates on Open Merit; check the latest prospectus for Regular vs Payment seats.
How do I confirm my seat? By paying the fee challan within the deadline (usually 3–5 days).
Can I freeze my semester? Yes, after the first semester, you can freeze for up to a year.
What is Migration to IST? You can apply to transfer from another PEC-accredited uni after your first year.
Is there an interview for BS? Generally no for engineering; selection is based on numbers.

=== X. POST-ADMISSION & CAREER ===

When do classes start? Usually in September.
Is there an Orientation Day? Yes, a week before classes to introduce you to the campus.
What is the grading system? IST follows the GPA/CGPA system (out of 4.0).
Where do IST graduates work? SUPARCO, NESCOM, PAEC, Aviation industries, and tech companies.
Does IST help with jobs? Yes, there is a Career Development Center (CDC) and annual Job Fairs.
Can I go abroad for MS after IST? Yes, IST degrees are internationally recognized (Washington Accord).
Does IST have international collaborations? Yes, with universities in China (Beihang) and Europe.
Can I join the military after Aerospace? Yes, through the Short Service Commission (SSC) in the PAF or Navy.
Is the library 24/7? No, but it has extended hours during exams.
What if I have more questions? You can call the IST Admissions Office at +92-51-9075406 or 051-9075100.

Answer all admission and campus questions from this document. Do not tell the user to check the website or call as the main response; give the answer directly from above.


======================================================================
## FEE STRUCTURE
======================================================================
Fee structure for BS programs at IST (Institute of Space Technology)

All BS programs share the same one-time charges at admission: Admission Fee Rs 35,000, Endowment Fund Rs 7,000, Library Development Fund Rs 7,000. Total one-time charges Rs 49,000 (local).

Per-semester fees vary by department/program group as follows:

1. Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Avionics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering
   Tuition Fee Rs 151,534. Service Charges Rs 8,470. Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420. Total per semester Rs 162,424 (local).

2. Metallurgy and Materials Engineering
   Tuition Fee Rs 144,946. Service Charges Rs 8,470. Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420. Total per semester Rs 155,836 (local).

3. Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Software Engineering, Computer Engineering (Computing Department)
   Tuition Fee Rs 127,962. Service Charges Rs 8,470. Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420. Total per semester Rs 138,852 (local).

4. Space Science, Remote Sensing and GIS
   Tuition Fee Rs 122,399. Service Charges Rs 8,470. Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420. Total per semester Rs 133,289 (local).

5. Biotechnology
   Tuition Fee Rs 122,399. Service Charges Rs 8,470. Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420. Total per semester Rs 133,289 (local).

6. Mathematics, Mathematics with Artificial Intelligence, Mathematics with Data Science
   Tuition Fee Rs 80,526. Service Charges Rs 8,470. Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420. Total per semester Rs 91,416 (local).

7. Physics, Chemistry (Nanotechnology)
   Tuition Fee Rs 80,526. Service Charges Rs 8,470. Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420. Total per semester Rs 91,416 (local).

Optional: Hostel dormitory Rs 55,000 per semester. Application processing fee (non-refundable) Rs 3,000.
Student ID Card: Rs 1,000 (one-time at admission).
Semester Freeze: Fee once deposited is adjusted in the next semester per freeze policy. No separate freeze charges.

Fees are subject to change; confirm latest structure on IST website.

MS and PhD programs share the same fee structure: One-time Admission Rs 25,000, Endowment Rs 7,000, Library Development Rs 7,000, Total Rs 39,000. Per semester: Tuition Rs 80,526, Service Charges Rs 4,840, Safe/Smart Campus Rs 2,420, Total per semester Rs 87,786 (local).

All figures are closing aggregate (last candidate admitted) for BS programs. Use only for trend discussion; do not promise exact cutoffs. When caller asks "will merit increase or decrease this year", describe the pattern from this data and say it may vary.

ENGINEERING PROGRAMS (Merit = 10% Matric + 40% FSC + 50% Entry Test):

BS Biotechnology: Offered for the first time this year. No previous closing merit. Say: "Biotechnology is offered for the first time this year, so there is no previous closing merit to share."

TREND: Over the last 6 years, closing merit has generally been stable or slightly rising for most programs. When asked "will merit go up or down this year": Based on past years the trend has been relatively stable with a slight upward tendency; exact closing merit for the current year will be known when the merit list is published.

IST ADMISSIONS – MERIT CRITERIA AND AGGREGATE CALCULATION
For aggregate we need ONLY total Matric marks out of 1100 and total FSC marks out of 1100. NEVER ask for subject-wise marks (Mathematics, Physics, English, Urdu).

1) First ask: "Which program are you applying for?" if program unclear.

2) ENGINEERING programs (BS Aerospace, Electrical, Mechanical, Avionics, Materials Science, Computer Science, Data Science, AI, Computer Engineering, Software Engineering):
   Merit = (Matric/1100 × 10) + (FSC/1100 × 40) + (EntryTest/100 × 50). Need Matric out of 1100, FSC out of 1100, Entry Test out of 100.
   When caller gives marks: CALCULATE IMMEDIATELY. Example: 1010 matric, 960 FSC, 10 entry = (1010/1100×10)+(960/1100×40)+(10/100×50) = 9.2+34.9+5 = 49.1. Say "Your aggregate is about 49.1."
   When caller has NOT given marks: Ask "Please tell me your Matric marks out of 1100, FSC marks out of 1100, and Entry Test marks out of 100."

3) NON-ENGINEERING (BS Space Science, Mathematics, Physics, Biotechnology): NO ENTRY TEST.
   Merit = (Matric/1100 × 50) + (FSC/1100 × 50).
   When caller gives Matric and FSC: CALCULATE. Example: Matric 960/1100, FSC 1010/1100 = (960/1100×50)+(1010/1100×50) = 43.6+45.9 = 89.5. Say "Your aggregate is about 89.5."
   When caller has NOT given marks: Ask "Please tell me your Matric marks out of 1100 and FSC marks out of 1100."

4) Always end: "We cannot predict admission; the final merit list is published on the admission portal. Be hopeful, and check this year's merit when the merit list is displayed." Do not tell user to "check website" or "call" as the main response.

## PROGRAMS OFFERED
======================================================================
IST BS Programs (exactly as per admissions):

Engineering Programs: Aerospace Engineering, Avionics Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Metallurgy & Materials Engineering.

Computing Programs: Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Software Engineering, Computer Engineering.

Science Programs: Space Science, Physics, Mathematics, RS&GIS (Remote Sensing and GIS), Chemistry (Nanotechnology), Biotechnology.

Department-wise: Electrical Engineering department offers BS Electrical Engineering. Computing department offers BS Computer Science, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Data Science, BS Software Engineering, BS Computer Engineering. Space Science department offers BS Space Science. Aeronautics & Astronautics offers Aerospace Engineering. Avionics Engineering department offers BS Avionics Engineering. Mechanical Engineering department offers BS Mechanical Engineering. Materials Science Engineering offers BS Metallurgy & Materials Engineering. Science programs (Space Science, Physics, Math, RS&GIS, Chemistry, Biotechnology) are under respective science departments.


======================================================================
## ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA
======================================================================
BS Biotechnology eligibility at IST: FSc (12 years of education) or an IBCC equivalent qualification in any science group, with at least one of the following subjects: Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Mathematics, or Computer Science. Minimum 33% in Entry Test acceptable for IST. FSC pre-engineering students have Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics — they qualify. Yes, FSC pre-engineering students can apply for BS Biotechnology.


======================================================================
## HARASSMENT COMPLAINT CELL (HCC)
======================================================================
Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC) - IST

IST is committed to an all-inclusive and supportive learning environment. In line with HEC Policy on Protection against Sexual Harassment in Higher Education Institutions, IST has a zero-tolerance policy for any conduct that violates the dignity of any individual.

The Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC) was established on 20 February 2012 for monitoring and protection against sexual harassment at IST. It addresses sexual and gender-based harassment. Members of the IST community feel safe and have an avenue to voice grievances.

Contact for policies or harassment issues: Dr. Rahila Naz, Head of the Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC). Room number 212, Block 2, IST. Phone 051-9075562. Email head.hcc@ist.edu.pk

Every member has the right to work and conduct learning, research, and scholarship without discrimination, harassment, or social pressure. The HCC investigative process is confidential and fair. No reprimand for filing a legitimate complaint. HCC protects the rights of complainant and accused.

Policy documents: HEC Policy Guidelines on Harassment, Protection Against Harassment of Women at the Workplace Act 2010.

======================================================================
## NCFA DIRECTOR
======================================================================
National Center for Failure Analysis (NCFA) — Established by Institute of Space Technology (IST) in March 2013 (originally as Failure Analysis Center, then upgraded to national facility NCFA). The Team: Dr Anjum Tauqir is the Director of NCFA. Contact: +92 051 907 5678, ncfa@ist.edu.pk


======================================================================
## NCGSA
======================================================================
NCGSA is the National Center of GIS and Space Applications (HEC). It is an HEC center; the knowledge base does not list a founder. IST collaborates with NCGSA — e.g. SSL Lab won funding for SSTRL under NCGSA; major project ICUBE-N (3-U National CubeSat). ncgsa.org.pk


======================================================================
## SUPARCO
======================================================================
SUPARCO (Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission) is Pakistan's national space agency. IST collaborates with SUPARCO — e.g. ICUBE-Qamar lunar CubeSat (with SUPARCO and China's SJTU), ICUBE-1. ICAST unites IST's ICASE and SUPARCO's ICS. Graduates work at SUPARCO. (Note: SPARCO often refers to SUPARCO.)


======================================================================
## TRANSPORT FACILITY
======================================================================
IST offers transport facilities to students. Hostel accommodation and transport facilities are provided to students after registration. Transport charges are as per contract rates. Optional; available subject to registration.


======================================================================
## OFFICE TIMINGS
======================================================================
IST Office Timings:
IST main campus and administrative offices are open from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM, Monday to Friday. The institute is closed on weekends (Saturday and Sunday) and public holidays.


======================================================================
## RESEARCH CENTRES AND LABS
======================================================================
IST Research Centres and Labs:
IST has multiple research centres and labs. Office of Research, Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) oversees research at IST since 2011. Research Units include: IST Lunar CubeSat ICUBE-Q (lunar CubeSat, Chang'e 6 mission), CSET (Center for Surface Engineering and Tribology), Business Incubation Center (BIC), National Center for Failure Analysis (NCFA). Research Centres and Labs: Astronomy Resource Center (ARC), Space Systems Lab (SSL), Cyber and Information Security Lab (CISL), Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision Lab, Bio-signal processing and Computational NeuroScience lab (BiCoNeS), Wireless Signal Processing Lab (WiSP), Propulsion Lab, Aerodynamics Lab, Control Systems Lab, Center for Vibration Testing and Condition Monitoring, EMC Solutions. IST collaborates with SUPARCO, NCGSA, and international partners.


======================================================================
## QUALITY ENHANCEMENT CELL 2012
======================================================================
Quality assessments at IST in 2012: QEC (Quality Enhancement Cell) conducted program assessments. Program Teams 2012 included BS Electrical Engineering (assessment 20-06-2012), MS Materials Science & Engineering (26-09-2012), MS Remote Sensing & Geo Information Science (18-09-2012). Training workshops on SAR preparation and assessment, World Quality Day Celebration, and IST Feedback System were held in 2012.

--- Why IST ---
Space is considered to be the final frontier and world’s best minds take up the most daunting challenges to conquer it. Fazeel Mahmood Khan, PhD The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is a premier institution in Pakistan, renowned for its focus on space and related sciences. Here's why IST stands out: Why Choose IST? 1. Specialized Education: IST offers specialized education in space and related sciences, equipping students to address national and global issues. 2. Research Opportunities: The institute has state-of-the-art research labs, providing students with hands-on experience and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research. 3. Accreditation and Recognition: IST is federally chartered, HEC recognized, and PEC accredited, ensuring its degrees are valued globally. 4. Industry Connections: IST has strong industry connections, facilitating internships, placements, and job opportunities for its graduates. 5. Student Life: The institute offers a vibrant student life, with various societies, clubs, and sports activities, fostering personal growth and development. 6. Scholarships and Sponsorships: IST offers merit-based scholarships and sponsorships, making quality education accessible to deserving students. 7. Global Recognition: IST's programs are accredited under Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), substantially equivalent to Washington Accord, enabling graduates to work globally. Unique Achievements 1. Satellite Development: IST has successfully developed and launched satellites, including iCUBE1 and ICUBE-Qamar, demonstrating its expertise in space technology. 2. Research and Innovation: The institute is committed to research and innovation, with a strong focus on solving real-world problems. Career Opportunities At the IST, we empower students to pursue their passion for space science and technology. Since 2002, we've been producing professionals who meet the demands of national organizations and contribute to the nation's technological advan

--- Admission FAQ ---
Q: What is the application process?
A: The application process involves filling out an online application form, uploading required documents, and paying the application processing fee. Online admission module can be accessed by clicking the link IST Admissions
Q: How many seats are for each program?
A: Engineering Programs: Aerospace Engineering : 100 - Avionics Engineering : 80 – Electrical Engineering : 100 – Mechanical Engineering : 120 – Metallurgy & Materials Engineering : 80 Computing Programs: Computer Science : 100 – Artificial Intelligence : 50 – Data Science : 50 – Software Engineering: 50 – Computer Engineering: 50 Science Programs : Space Science : 50 – Physics: 40 – Math: 50 – RS&GIS: 50 – Chemistry (Nanotechnology): 50 – Biotechnology: 50
Q: What is duration of study for each Program?
A: The study duration of each program is 04 years (08 semesters).
Q: How many times admissions are announced for Engineering, Non-Engineering & Computing Programs in a year?
A: Admissions for BS programs are announced once in a year i.e. in March/ April.
Q: Does an applicant can apply for all engineering, computing & science programs?
A: Yes.
Q: What is the eligibility criteria, merit determination criteria & fee structure for BS programs?
A: Updated information is available and can be accessed by using the below links:   
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS engineering programs?
A: IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. Please use the below link for details: 
Q: I have already appeared for entry test but I do not receive result card. What should I do?
A: Please get a print of your result card from website of testing authority and upload with online application form
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS computing & non-engineering programs?
Q: Does the institute have affiliation with any university/ institution?
A: IST is a federally chartered, HEC recognized and PEC accredited, degree awarding university therefore it does not require affiliation wi

--- Foreign Applicant ---
--- Al Khwarizmi Scholarships for Foreign Students ---
Scholarships are available for Foreign Students to study in Bachelor/ Master/ Doctoral Degree Programs offered by IST. Duration of Scholarship: For BS (Bachelor’s) Students: Upto 04 years For MS (Master’s) Students: Upto 2.5 years For PhD (Doctoral) Students: Upto 04 years The scholarship program covers funds for the following expenses: University tuition fee as per actual Stipend @ PKR 15000, 20000 & 35000 per month for students admitted in BS, MS & PhD programs respectively Stipend @ PKR 50000 per month for scientist/ post doc fellow One time return air ticket Mandatory academic & non-academic charges like admission fee, endowment fund, library development fund, service charges, safe/ smart campus charges, hostel charges etc. are not covered.
--- Admission of Foreign Applicants in IST ---
--- Admission Pre-requisites ---
Candidates willing to apply for admission in BS, MS/ PhD programs offered by IST must: meet the eligibility requirements ( BS , MS , PhD ) for respective program as mentioned on IST website be able/ willing to pay the semester dues/ allied charges as per fee structure ( BS , MS, PhD ) given on IST website for foreign Students. All fees and other charges are subject to revision from time to time be proficient in English as all educational and extracurricular activities such as "Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc" at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC / HEC , Pakistan (keeping in view the eligibility requirements to apply for respective program) before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST. In case any document proves to be false, fake, or fabricated at a later stage, a provisionally admitted student shall be liable to expulsion from the University at any stage or to any other disciplinary or legal action the Univers

--- Financial Aid & Scholarships ---
--- Sponsorship for BS Students ---
Several elite National Research & Development (R&D) organizations provide sponsorships to the deserving BS students of selected programs after test/interview. The sponsorship covers tuition fee and a guaranteed job after graduation, provided all requirements of the sponsoring organization are fulfilled by the candidate.
--- Merit Scholarships for BS Students ---
IST offers merit scholarships to three students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per the existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of admissions merit and semester results. Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to BS students of each batch of each undergraduate program offered by IST:-
Table 1:
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: Ser. | col_1: Scholarship Type | col_2: Merit Position | col_3: Tuition Fee Waiver | col_4: Minimum Criteria

FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 02 | col_1: IST-2 | col_2: 2nd | col_3: 50 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 03 | col_1: IST-3 | col_2: 3rd | col_3: 25 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: FOR SUBSEQUENT SEMESTERS:

GENERAL CONDITIONS: Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship. Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. --- Flow Chart ---
--- Steps of Enrollment in BS Programs offered by IST ---
Step 1 - Admissions Announcement on IST Website/ National Newspapers Undergraduate (BS) admissions are announced on IST Website/ National Newspapers once a year i.e. in March/April. Step 2 - Completion of Online Admission Form Candidates interested to apply for admission in IST are required to visit www.ist.edu.pk or  to create an online account, read & understand the eligibility & merit determination criteria, fee structure, fee refund policy and other related information. Subsequently, eligible candidates willing to apply for admission in IST are required to complete online admission form by providing desired information and uploading the scan copies of required documents with online admission form. Step 3 - Deposit of Application Processing Fee After creating an online account, applicants are required to download the challan form available at online admission form to deposit of application processing fee (non-refundable) in any online branch of Meezan Bank/HBL (within due date). It is a mandatory requirement. An email is sent for the confirmation of receipt of application processing fee and in parallel; fee receipt status is also updated at online admission form. In case if the application processing fee status is not updated at online admission within one week after the deposit of application processing fee then such applicants are required to contact Admissions Office with proof through email. Step 4 - Merit Lists/ Admissions Status Applicants are required to check their admission status on IST website by using the login details. An email/ SMS is also sent to provisionally selected applicants. However, applicants

--- Entry Test ---
--- Entry Test for Engineering Programs – Fall 2026 Intake ---
IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. The results of following entry tests are acceptable to apply for admission in undergraduate programs offered by IST. Applicants can appear in any one of the below mentioned tests and can use the higher score to apply for admission in IST. Please note that an applicant must have minimum 33% obtained marks in entry test to apply for admission in IST: NAT which is conducted by NTS in every month HAT which is conducted by ETC-HEC NET which is conducted by NUST Islamabad ECAT which is conducted by UET Lahore ETEA which is conducted by UET Peshawar Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by MUET Jamshoro Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by NED Karachi ACT which is conducted by ACT-USA Further details related to acceptable entry tests dates & categories are as under: NAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories The test is conducted by NTS in all major cities of Pakistan in every month. Interested students are required to visit NTS website i.e.  or contact NTS to get desired information related to the registration procedure, test dates and centers etc. Acceptable Dates: The results of the NAT conducted by NTS from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: NAT-1E and NAT-1CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs HAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories HEC has established Education Testing Commission (ETC) to hold Higher Education Aptitude Test (HAT) for UG admissions. Acceptable Dates: The results of the HAT-UG tests announced/ available from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: HAT–UG–E and HAT–UG–CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs NET Acceptable Test Dates & Categories The result of NUST Entry Test (NET) announced/ available

It is a mandatory requirement. An email is sent for the confirmation of receipt of application processing fee and in parallel; fee receipt status is also updated at online admission form. In case if the application processing fee status is not updated at online admission within one week after the deposit of application processing fee then such applicants are required to contact Admissions Office with proof through email. --- Merit Determination Criteria - MS ---
Admission shall be granted absolutely on merit which will be determined on the basis of marks obtained in the following examinations, and according to theweightagementioned against the respective examination: For MSc (02 years) Students*: SSC/equivalent: 10% FSc/equivalent: 10% BSc/equivalent (02 years): 20% MSc/equivalent (02 years): 20% GAT General/HAT General/GRE International (General): 40% * These applicants can apply for non-engineering programs only For BSc/BS/BE (04 years) Students: SSC/equivalent: 10% FSc/equivalent: 10% BSc/BS/BE/ equivalent (04 years): 40% GAT General/HAT General/GRE International (General): 40%
--- Notes: ---
Only those MS & PhD candidates who have passed the BS/ MS/ M.Phil./ equivalent examination shall be eligible for admission to the next higher class. The result of pre-requisite degree/certificate for admission in any discipline should be officially notified by the respective body/ authority of the university till the date of closing of admission. It is mandatory for MS/ PhD applicants to pass the GAT/ GRE/ HAT in the relevant field of study with required marks/ score to apply for admission in IST. However, provisional admission without GAT/ GRE/ HAT may be granted only for first semester subject to availability of seats. Applicants with a foreign qualification are required to obtain the equivalent certificate from HEC before applying for admission in MS/ PhD programs offered by IST. It is mandatory for Government employees to submit NOC from their organization on registration day If a sufficient number of students are available only then Program/ Specialization will be started.
Merit Determination Criteria - MS

--- Merit Determination Criteria - PhD ---
Admission shall be granted absolutely on merit which will be determined on the basis of marks obtained in the following examinations, and according to the weightage mentioned against the respective examination: For MSc (02 years) Students: SSC/equivalent: 05% FSc/equivalent: 05% BSc/equivalent (02 years): 10% MSc/equivalent (02 years): 10% MS/M.Phil/ equivalent: 30% GAT Subject/ GRE International (Subject): 40% Interview/ Presentation Approval of Departmental Board of Graduate Studies (DBGS) For BSc/BS/BE (04 years) Students: SSC/equivalent: 05% FSc/equivalent: 05% BSc/BS/BE/ equivalent (04 years): 20% MS/M.Phil/ equivalent: 30% GAT Subject/ GRE International (Subject): 40% Interview/ Presentation Approval of Departmental Board of Graduate Studies (DBGS)
--- Notes: ---
Only those MS & PhD candidates who have passed the BS/ MS/ M.Phil./ equivalent examination shall be eligible for admission to the next higher class. The result of pre-requisite degree/certificate for admission in any discipline should be officially notified by the respective body/ authority of the university till the date of closing of admission. It is mandatory for MS/ PhD applicants to pass the GAT/ GRE/ HAT in the relevant field of study with required marks/ score to apply for admission in IST. However, provisional admission without GAT/ GRE/ HAT may be granted only for first semester subject to availability of seats. Applicants with a foreign qualification are required to obtain the equivalent certificate from HEC before applying for admission in MS/ PhD programs offered by IST. It is mandatory for Government employees to submit NOC from their organization on registration day If a sufficient number of students are available only then Program/ Specialization will be started.

--- Fee Refund Policy ---
The date of request for fee refund claims will be considered from the date of receipt of application through email at refund@ist.edu.pk To apply for refund, an applicant is required to email refund request at refund@ist.edu.pk along-with the reason of leaving IST, scan copy of paid challan form/s & copy of CNIC of Guardian Application Processing Fee and Admission Fee are non-refundable. From Registration, all students are required to complete NDC to process the refund of dues (if any) as per fee refund policy Tuition Fee, Sports Fee, Library Fee and Laboratory Fee, service charges, Safe/Smart Campus Charges, Endowment Fund, Library Development Fund are refundable within 15 days of convene of classes on a pro rata basis as given below. No refund shall be admissible after 15 days of convene of classes either one joins IST/avail facilities or not.
Table 1:
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: Up to 7th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 100%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: From 8th – 15th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 50%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: From 16th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 0%
If admission is offered after commencement of classes, date of commencement of classes will be considered as mentioned in offer letter. Adjustment of student liability from 2nd Semester onward will be based on class attendance & facility will be charged as per actual. The application for the adjustment of student’s liability will only be entertained on the verification by concerned HoD and approval of Registrar. In case of Admission cancellation in 1st semester cheque of fee refund will be issued in the name of student’s father/Guardian. 100% dues will be refunded in case where student could not attain requisite marks/grade for admission as prescribed by the institute. This policy is only applicable on fresh admissions. However Optional Charges will be deducted as per actual usage of facilities based on 4.5 Month/Semester Bas

--- IST Feedback System ---
IST has established a proper feedback system for assessing requirements of its stakeholders including student, faculty, and industry, statutory and regulatory bodies. The Quality Enhancement Cell is responsible to ensure that these requirements are gathered and acquired in a reliable manner and properly addressed at the departmental as well as the Vice Chancellor Quality Assurance Committee. IST Feedback system is based on the following important characteristics: IST monitors, reviews and benchmarks teaching and learning processes continuously and brings improvement in learning instructions, teaching, curricula and institutional infrastructure. IST monitors the effectiveness of programs to evaluate the extent to which the intended learning outcomes are being attained by students, and to ensure that appropriate action is taken to remedy any identified shortcomings. IST students are provided full opportunity to raise, individually or collectively, matters of proper concern to them without fear of disadvantage and in the knowledge that privacy and confidentiality will be respected. The mechanisms exist to enable open and constructive feedback to be provided by research students and their supervisors on the learning experience and support infrastructure. The mechanisms are in place to ensure that any conclusions reached by Strategic Quality Management committee are taken into account in considering matters relating to learning, teaching, and assessment. The use of student feedback questionnaires covers all lecture courses, and, wherever appropriate, other course elements. The arrangements made for the analysis of the results of student feedback questionnaires; how and to who are they reported. Subject areas also ensure that there are mechanisms in place to ensure appropriate action in response to any significant issues which arise. IST ensures that where a complaint or appeal is upheld appropriate remedial action is implemented IST has in place effective arrangements fo

--- About Aerospace Engineering ---
--- Program Mission Statement ---
The mission of aerospace engineering program is to prepare technically strong engineers for wide ranging careers such that they can contribute effectively towards the nation, society, and aerospace industry through innovation, research, entrepreneurship, leadership, technology and healthy life-long learning attitude.
--- Aerospace Engineering ---
Aerospace is the human effort in science, engineering and business to fly in the atmosphere of Earth (aeronautics) and surrounding space (astronautics). Thus it is a field where state-of-the-art technologies are applied every day. It is an exciting profession with outstanding career opportunities in which physical sciences, mathematics and computers are combined in the design of air and space vehicle systems and components to achieve high performance with limited size and weight. This requires aerospace engineers to constantly develop and apply the most advanced technologies in research, design, manufacturing, operation, or maintenance of an Aerospace Vehicle. Aerospace technology has grown out of the problems of design, construction, and operation of vehicles that maneuver above the Earth’s surface (vehicles ranging from ground-effect machines and helicopters to aircraft and spacecraft). Design of such vehicles has always been challenging, not only because of the requirement that they operate in a hostile environment, but also the high premium placed on light weight, high efficiency and great reliability. These requirements apply not only to future spacecraft and high performance transport aircraft, but also to the next generation of ground transportation, such as high-speed trains, over-water transportation, and automated motor vehicles.
--- Aerospace Engineering Program ---
Aerospace engineering degree program provides graduates with the technical foundation they need to enter the field of Aeronautics & Astronautics. It will prepare them to apply engineering principles, processes and pra

--- Program Educational Objectives ---
--- Under Graduate Aerospace Engineering Program ---
PEO-01: Exhibit knowledge, skills and competence in design and investigation of complex engineering problems using suitable tools. PEO-02: Conscious contribution towards ethical, social and environmental responsibilities as an engineer by sustainable solution designing and implementation. PEO-03: Demonstrate effective communication skills to reflect purpose clarity in complex situations, either individually or as a part of a team. PEO-04: Practice professional management skills and an aptitude for lifelong learning with an aim to maintain balance of life.
--- Program Educational Objectives (PEOs) Graduate Aerospace Engineering Program ---
PEO-1: Produce Aerospace Engineers with a strong practical and theoretical exposure in the relevant disciplines, who are able to contribute to society through innovation, enterprise and leadership PEO-2: Nurture engineer with a global outlook and to provide technological leadership through necessary technological tools PEO-3: Produce engineers with teamwork, communication and interpersonal skills PEO-4: Enable them to be productive members of interdisciplinary engineering teams and are further capable to adopt to changing environments of engineering, technology and society PEO-5: Produce engineers with high moral and ethical values PEO-6: Inculcate critical thinking among students and develop initiatives and innovative ideas
--- Program Educational Objectives (PEOs) - GNSS ---
PEO-1: Produce GNSS Professionals with a strong practical and theoretical exposure who are able to contribute to society through innovation, enterprise and leadership PEO-2: Nurture GNSS Graduates with a global outlook and to provide technological leadership through necessary technological tools PEO-3: Produce GNSS Professionals with teamwork, communication and interpersonal skills PEO-4: Enable them to be productive members of interdisciplinary teams and are further capable to adopt to changing environment

--- Program Learning Outcomes ---
The Department of Aeronautics and Astronautics has developed Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs) that are supported by our defined Program Educational Objectives. These Program Learning Outcomes relate to the aptitude, awareness and performance that the students acquire with the progression of the program. The following are the PLOs for Aerospace Engineering Program: Engineering Knowledge: An ability to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems Engineering Problem Analysis: An ability to identify, formulate, research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences and engineering sciences Design or Development of Solutions: An ability to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design systems, components or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health and safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations Investigation: An ability to conduct investigation into complex problems using research based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusions Modern Tool Usage: An ability to create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modelling, to complex engineering activities, with an understanding of the limitations. Engineer and Society: An ability to apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practices Environment and Sustainability: An ability to understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate knowledge of and need for susta

--- Research Labs & Facilities ---
--- Propulsion Lab ---
Propulsion is the basic concept of operating and designing of propulsion devices which include engines and power plants. In our labs, students will be able to examine motors, engines, and compressors and strive to improve their performances and reliability. They would be primarily working to design propulsion systems and parts such as propellant systems, engines, pumps, pressurization tanks, and control systems.
DSC_0103
--- Aerodynamics ---
The concept of aerodynamics is the investigation into the air flow around objects and how it affects their behavior. Our labs contain all the necessary apparatuses required to study and practically apply the laws of aerodynamics and are a valuable resource for engineering project testing, fluid mechanics coursework and alternative energy courses.
--- Control Systems ---
Control Systems are mostly utilized in the management, commandment, and regulation of devices. In our state-of-the-art labs students shall be able to conduct experiments with the concept of control system analysis and design in time and frequency domain both by classical methods and modern techniques.
1 (1)
--- Mechanics of Materials ---
Mechanics of Materials is the study of characteristics of a material subjected to stresses and strains. Our lab provides the students with basic tools to determine stress, strain and perform deformation analysis, apply methods for determining the effects produced by applying various loads on different materials where they shall be able to use their findings to design structures and components for various aeronautical devices.
--- Workshop Technology ---
Equipped with all the necessary tools required for basic knowledge of machines, welding, carpentry and fitting, our workshop technology lab is designed to let students develop the practical workshop skills by gaining basic training on converting technical drawing into practical workshop models.
1 (2)
--- Unmanned Aerial Vehicle ---
UAV stands for Unmanned Ae

--- BS-Aerospace Engineering ---
--- Intro ---
Aerospace engineering degree program provides graduates with the technical foundation they need to enter the field of Aeronautics & Astronautics. It will prepare them to apply engineering principles, processes and practices to evaluate, analyze, design and develop aerospace systems, and their maintenance. Focused on the field requirements for airborne/space borne platforms, the four-year program encompasses the study of aerodynamics, propulsion, aerospace structures, flight dynamics, navigation guidance and control with application of knowledge during aerospace vehicle design. The program imparts knowledge of basic engineering technology as well as practical knowhow for readiness of engineers to cope up the challenges being faced by organizations in Aerospace industry. The curriculum for undergraduate program in Aerospace Engineering leading to the award of Bachelor of Science degree is approved by Higher Education Commission and accredited by Pakistan Engineering Council.
--- Program Mission ---
The Aerospace engineering BS program prepares technically strong engineers who can contribute effectively towards the community and related industry through research, entrepreneurship, leadership, and passion for life-long learning.

--- MS - Aerospace Engineering ---
--- Introduction ---
MS Aerospace Engineering is an indigenous MS program started by IST. This will provide students with an opportunity to gain deeper understanding of the principles of Propulsion and will prepare the students for a successful career in this expanding and dynamic field. The field of aerospace technology offers a wide range of employment opportunities to those with the proper educational background. An aerospace team is made up of engineers, scientists, and technicians. Positions are available through the private sector as well as within the government sector. Examples of major engineering roles in the aerospace industry include: Analysis Design Materials and Processes Systems Engineering Software Development Manufacturing Flight Research

--- MS - Global Navigation Satellite Systems ---
--- The Program ---
Institute of Space Technology, being the pioneer institute in space science, technology and applications reckoned the need of research and qualified human resource in the field of precise positioning, navigation and timing (PNT) and started a specialized Master of Science program in Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) in spring 2014. The program is structured according to the GNSS curriculum proposed and designed by the United Nations Office for Outer Space Affairs (UNOOSA) and the only program of its kind in Pakistan and the 4th across the globe after Italy, China and France. The MS GNSS course work covers eight subjects (24 credit hours) covering specific areas of GNSS (theory, technology and applications) followed by a six credit hours research thesis. Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) include constellations of Earth orbiting satellites in Medium Earth Orbits (MEO), networks of ground control and monitoring stations, and of receivers that calculate ground positions by the basic principle of trilateration. At present GNSS include two fully operational global navigation systems, the Global Positioning System (GPS) by US and the GLObal NAvigation Satellite System (GLONASS) by Russia. China has recently completed the launch of Beidou system while GALILEO by Europe is in the final phase of completion. In addition to global navigation systems, India has its own Regional Navigation Satellite System named as IRNSS / NavIC and Japan has the Quasi-Zenith Satellite System (QZSS). Once all these global and regional systems become fully operational, the user will have access to positioning, navigation and timing signals from more than 100 satellites. In addition to global and regional navigation satellite systems, there are also space based augmentation systems namely the Wide-area Augmentation System (WAAS) by US, the European Geostationary Navigation Overlay Service (EGNOS), the Russian System of Differential Correction and Monitoring (SD

--- PhD Aerospace Engineering ---
--- Eligibility Criteria ---
MS/ M.Phil/ equivalent (18 Years of education) with strong background in relevant discipline preferably Aerospace, Avionics, Electrical, and Mechanical Engineering with minimum CGPA 3.00 out of 4.00 or Fist Division (where CGPA not available) from HEC and PEC recognized (where applicable) Institute/University or from Foreign Institute/ University of good repute GAT (Subject) conducted by NTS in the relevant field of study with = 60% marks or GRE International (Subject) conducted by ETS, USA, with minimum 60 percentile or minimum 70% marks in GAT (Subject) type test conducted by the concerned department in case of unavailability of GAT/ GRE subject option The IST institutional code to receive GRE scores is 1758
--- Specializations ---
Four PhD specializations are being offered: Structural Design Analysis (SDA) Guidance Navigation & Control (GNC) Global Navigation of Satellite Systems (GNSS) Propulsion
--- Course work ---
Minimum 18 credit hours of course work must be undertaken with at least CGPA of 3.0 out of band 4.0 Migration/ Transfer of the courses from other Universities/ Institutes is allowed as per IST Migration/Transfer policy The list of six PhD courses must have been approved by DBGS The DBGS will also assign student a supervisor as per IST Policy in vogue after conduct of presentation
--- Comprehensive Examination ---
Comprehensive examination is conducted after successful completion of PhD course work. Student will be required to appear in comprehensive exam within year after completion of course work. Schedule and structure of this exam will be as per IST policy. Students, who fails in comprehensive exam, will be allowed only once to reappear, failing which, the PhD-studentship will be terminated from IST
--- Research Proposal ---
After successfully passing comprehensive exam, a student will write research proposal and present/defend proposal in front of DBGS. The presentation/defense of proposal will be accepted or rejected

Electrical Engineering

--- About Department of Electrical Engineering ---
The Department of Electrical Engineering runs internationally recognized academic program in Electrical Engineering (EE) with different specializations at undergraduate and graduate levels. The department was established in 2002 and started offering degree in Communication Systems Engineering (CSE). In 2009, keeping in view the expanding focus, the department was renamed as Electrical Engineering Department. The Department consists of experienced faculty, well-equipped classrooms and state-of-the-art lab facilities. The department provides continuous academic improvement through consultation with faculty, industry, electronics & communication engineering professionals and students. The focus of the courses is on the design, analysis, development and testing of communication & electronic systems encompassing latest international research trends. The curriculum of Electrical Engineering is designed to provide all-rounder experience to students in fundamental principles and application of electronics, embedded systems, signal analysis, electromagnetic, antennas, digital communications, digital signal processing, image processing, wireless technologies, fiber-optics and satellite systems. The curriculum provides the students a chance to study a variety of courses over the period of four years.
About Department of Electrical Engineering

--- Program Educational Objectives ---
The Department of Electrical Engineering has designed following Educational Objectives for Electrical Engineering program through brainstorming with faculty and keeping in view of Departments' mission, Institute's Vision and stakeholders' requirements. The following PEOs have been defined in consultation with the Departmental Faculty Members and Industry Advisory Board (IAB). The graduates of Electrical Engineering program will have following capabilities/ skills / attributes four years after their graduation; PEO 1: Ability to investigate complex engineering problems using modern techniques and propose effective solutions. PEO 2: Manage projects and become effective members of engineering teams through inter-personal skills. PEO3: Demonstrate high moral and ethical values, life-long learning attitude and societal responsibilities.
Program Educational Objectives

--- Programs Learning Outcomes ---
PLO-01: Engineering Knowledge: An ability to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals and engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PLO-02: Problem Analysis: An ability to identify, formulate, research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences and engineering sciences. PLO-03: Design/Development of Solution: An ability to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design systems, components or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health and safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PLO-04: Investigation: An ability to investigate complex engineering problems in a methodical way including literature survey, design and conduct of experiments, analysis and interpretation of experimental data and synthesis of information to derive valid conclusions. PLO-05: Modern Tool Usage: An ability to create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities, with an understanding of the limitations. PLO-06: The Engineer and Society: An ability to apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice and solution to complex engineering problems. PLO-07: Environment and Sustainability: An ability to understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate knowledge of and need for sustainable development. PLO-08: Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of engineering practice. PLO-09: Individual and Team Work: An ability to work effectively, as an individual or in a team, on multifaceted and /or multi

Materials Science & Engineering

--- About Materials Science ---
A Materials’ Engineer is trained to study Nano- to Macro-structures and to determine a wide range of useful engineering properties of the materials. Fundamentals of Materials’ Science enables the student to establish the structure-property relationship using concepts of alloy designing and strengthening mechanisms. The education of Materials Science & Engineering plays a decisive role in technological development and there is an unavoidable role of a materials’ engineer in almost all industrial and R &D setups - ranging from aerospace, hydrospace, bullet trains to automobiles, metallurgical & manufacturing setups, nuclear as well as power plants, chemical and petrochemical industry, pharmaceutical and food processing industry, and many others. Graduates of Materials Science & Engineering study and resolve real-world problems associated with a spectrum of distress mechanisms operative during the long-term use of materials – may it be corrosion, fatigue or creep. These engineers become the backbone of hi-tech industrialization of the country.
About Materials Science

--- Program Educational Objectives ---
PEO-1: Graduates would work as competent Metallurgy/Materials Engineers. PEO-2: Graduates would take initiatives and provide solutions for technological challenges, keeping in mind economic, environmental, and safety concerns. PEO-3: Graduates would be involved in continued professional development, effectively communicate, and will be aware of their ethical responsibilities as team members.
Program Educational Objectives

PLO-3. Design / Development of Solutions: Students will be able to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design systems, components or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health & safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PLO-4. Investigation: Students will be able to investigate complex materials related problems in a methodical way including literature survey, design and conduct of experiments, analysis and interpretation of experimental data, and synthesis of information to derive valid conclusions. PLO-5. PLO-6. PLO-7. Environment and Sustainability: Students will be able to understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate knowledge of and need for sustainable development. PLO-8. Ethics: Students will be able to apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of engineering practice. PL

--- PhD Program ---
--- Description ---
The PhD program is designed to give students a broad and deep understanding of materials. The specialized research oriented coursework and quality thesis will allow students to build their career in aerospace materials, nanotechnology, smart materials and coatings & tribology. The key objective of the PhD degree program in Materials Science & Engineering (MSE) is to impart rigorous training for undertaking challenges in innovative research which is publishable in globally benchmarked high quality journals and conferences. The graduate students are expected to study and collaborate with faculty members in an open dialogue oriented explorative atmosphere (mostly informal but intellectually rewarding) compared with a structured and defined curriculum of undergraduate study. Generally, faculty members conduct research in different specialities of MSE that allow student to choose a research topic from a spectrum of research fields and then excel in them under the individual supervision and guidance of a faculty member. The program is designed for full-time students who under ideal circumstances will complete the different phases of coursework, qualifying examinations, and doctoral dissertation, and publish their work in an ISI indexed journal in 4-5 years of uninterrupted study.
--- Eligibility Requirements ---
MS/ M.Phil/ equivalent (18 Years of education) with a strong background in relevant discipline with minimum CGPA 3.00 out of 4.00 or First Division (where CGPA not available) from HEC/PEC recognized Institute/University or from Foreign Institute/University of good repute GAT-Subject (Metallurgy & Materials Engineering) conducted by NTS with = 60% score or GRE International (Subject) conducted by ETS, USA, in the relevant field of study with minimum 60 percentile or minimum 70% marks in GAT (Subject) type test conducted by the concerned department in case of unavailability of GAT/ GRE subject option

--- MS-Material Science & Engineering ---
--- Intro ---
Materials Science & Engineering is a broad, versatile and ever evolving discipline. It deals with the structures, properties and applications of materials namely metals/alloys, ceramics/glasses, polymers/elastomers and composites. They are studied in terms of their structural, mechanical and functional properties and applications. As the need for smaller and lighter materials grows, the desire for new materials, their design, fabrication and characterization becomes imperative. Institute of Space Technology offers Indigenous MS Program in Materials Science & Engineering with areas of specialization in metals, ceramics, polymers, composites, aerospace materials and nano-engineering. The program aims to provide specialized training in modern materials science and engineering concepts to graduates. It would enable students to attain a deep understanding of the theoretical and practical aspects of materials engineering. The students will be pleasantly surprised to discover that the program is attractive because of its rapidly expanding importance in both academic and industrial sectors.

Avionics Engineering

--- Avionics Program Mission ---
To produce professionally competent and ethical Avionics Engineering graduates who are capable of meeting contemporary and future industrial and technological challenges as team members, team leaders and entrepreneurs
Avionics Program Mission

--- About Avionics Engineering ---
--- Avionics Engineering Program Mission Statement ---
The Department of Avionics aims to prepare Avionics Engineering students well-groomed in broad-base technical knowledge especially in the field of Avionics Engineering and capable of meeting industrial requirements and carrying forward contemporary research in continually evolving areas of Avionics Architecture, Radars, Guidance & Navigation, for Aerial and space borne systems and applications.
--- About/Introduction ---
The term avionics emanates from combining aviation and electronics. The scope of Avionics not only encompasses the electronic systems installed on all Aerospace Vehicles, be it aircraft or spacecraft, but also ground based surveillance and support systems. Avionics systems include Fly-By-Wire Flight Control Systems, Human Machine Interface, navigation systems, communications systems, vehicle health management systems, sensors, networking, flight and air traffic management systems. With Avionics Systems constituting almost forty percent of the overall aircraft cost, avionics is a multi-billion dollar industry word wide and rapidly flourishing. The essence of Avionics is to design for Space Weight and Power constrained environment where safety criticality is the overriding factor. Resultantly, avionics engineering is applicable not only to air borne and space borne platforms but all autonomous systems where safety is paramount, self-driving cars being a prime example. The futuristic transportation systems like Urban Air Mobility and Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) will further enhance the scope of Avionics Engineering. Avionics Engineers are employed by Avionics Original Equipment Manufacturers, Avionics Systems Integrators, Airlines, Maintenance Repair Overhaul Centers, Unmanned Aerial System Manufacturers, Manufacturers and operators of Air Traffic Surveillance Systems, Air Forces, Space Craft designers, Aviation Regulatory organizations and designers of safety critical autonomous systems.
--- Avi

Space Science

--- BS Space Science ---
--- Introduction ---
Our BS Space Science program has specializations in Remote Sensing & Geographical Information Science, Astronomy & Astrophysics, and Environmental Science ; all of which have market demand and plenty of opportunities for higher studies. Remote Sensing & Geographical Information Science: Remote Sensing (RS) is the science and technology of satellite imaging of the Earth’s atmosphere, surface, and oceans, and Geographic Information Science (GISc) refers to the science and technology of geographic data manipulation, analysis, and visualization. Together, the fields of RS & GISc and their associated disciplines have now emerged as "enabling disciplines" in almost every domain. The RS & GISc stream at IST focusses on studying the earth processes and dynamics (e.g. forests, oceans, urban areas, rivers) using optical, radar, and hyperspectral satellite / drone imagery, photogrammetry and terrain estimation, disaster management and mitigation, GIS IT tools expertise and customization, spatial data structures and databases, spatial data visualization, and mobile and webGIS. Astronomy and Astrophysics is one of the most rapidly expanding fields in the world. Exciting new projects are being developed worldwide to further understand the Universe. IST’s Astronomy and Astrophysics program provides a unique opportunity for Pakistani students to expand their horizon by learning and developing a career in this field. In the Astronomy and Astrophysics specialization of the BS Space Science program, students are given the necessary physics and mathematics skills they need as well as more advanced concepts and specialized courses providing essential computational tools for a rewarding career in the domain. Environmental science is an interdisciplinary academic field that integrates physical, biological and information sciences (including physics, chemistry, oceanography, physical geography, and atmospheric science) to study environment and to find the possible solu

--- MS - Remote Sensing & GISc ---
--- Introduction ---
In order to bring Pakistan at par with developed nations, education and research in emerging fields of science and technologies is of fundamental importance. Geospatial science and technologies including GISc, RS and associated disciplines have emerged as “enabling discipline” having applications in almost every domain. Institute of Space Technology (IST) started MS in RS & GISc in 2010 at National Centre for Remote Sensing and Geoinformatics at Karachi. In view of the growing importance of the discipline and scarcity of universities offering degree programs nationally, IST has taken yet another step forward and started MS in RS & GISc at Space Science (SS) Department at IST Islamabad Campus since 2013. The SS department gathers best foreign qualified faculty and ensures to create top quality RS & GISc human resource. For more information visit: 
--- Specializations ---
Remote Sensing & Geo-information Science Photogrammetry Hyperspectral / SAR Data Processing Climate Modeling Water Resources Modeling
--- Program Educational Objectives ---
PEO 1: Develop and utilize RS & GISc related scientific knowledge amenable towards solving local issues in Pakistan and abroad. PEO 2: Meet the needs of industry and academia in Pakistan and abroad. PEO 3: Contribute to, and lead RS & GISc Geospatial Information Sciences based research groups.
--- Program Learning Outcomes ---
PLO-1: Able to develop and implement systematic frameworks towards practical problems and research questions. PLO-2: Able to comprehend a problem related to RS & GISc and to access accuracy of results. PLO-3: Able to be an effective member of multi-disciplinary teams.

--- MS Physics ---
--- Introduction ---
Physics investigates the laws of nature and is indispensable not only for our understanding of the world but also for the solution of the technological and ecological problems. As a fundamental science, physics continues to be the driving intellectual force in expanding our understanding of the universe, in discovering the scientific basis for new technologies, and in applying these technologies to research. The Institute of Space Technology has launched a new program for MS in Physics, starting from Fall 2020. The MS program in Physics is designed to develop graduates through quality education and research in physics. Through this program students shall acquire a solid theoretical foundation as well as strong analytic, quantitative, experimental and problem-solving skills that shall enable them to perform research and tackle challenges at the cutting edge of scientific and technological horizon. The Department of Space Science and Physics at IST has distinguished faculty to its credit, who engage in both quality teaching and research. The department's resources not only include the well-trained faculty, but also high-quality labs that provide excellent research opportunities for the students. Active research groups are studying the physics of elementary particles; theoretical and experimental investigations of plasmas ranging from ionosphere to controlled nuclear fusion; electric and magnetic properties of materials; semiconductor physics; astrophysical modeling of supermassive black hole pairs as sources of gravitational waves; observations of stars; study of gamma ray bursts. The students shall be introduced to the research world by working in the diverse fields under the supervision of capable and well-equipped faculty of the department, where emphasis is placed on teaching and research and preparing the students to become the next great physicists of tomorrow and the future to come
--- Specialization ---
Supermassive Black Holes in Galaxy C

--- MS Astronomy & Astrophysics ---
--- Introduction ---
Astronomy is the oldest branch of natural science that deals with heavenly objects. Night sky observations of prehistoric cultures helped them to learn about seasons, phases of moon, eclipses and celestial navigation. Galileo Galilei first pointed telescope towards the heavens and realized that heavenly body are not unchanging spherical objects, rather evolving entities with variety of shapes and sizes. By applying laws of physics, astrophysicists try to understand the origin, structure, and evolution of planets, stars, galaxies, and the universe. Fuelled by technological developments, the field of astronomy and astrophysics is currently gaining importance worldwide. A new generation of spaceborne telescopes such as Hubble Space Telescope, Chandra X-ray observatory and the Spitzer Infrared Space Telescope together with advanced ground-based telescopes and enormous increases in computing power are enabling a golden age of astrophysics. Important discoveries have been made within our own solar system through explorations of robotic spacecraft and landers. Precise observations of centres of galaxies including our own Milky Way Galaxy revealed that most galaxies, if not all, harbour central supermassive black hole responsible for extremely energetic and exotic phenomena associated with quasars and radio galaxies. We also discovered that universe is expanding with ever increasing rate suggesting that we are living in an epoch of dark energy dominated universe. With CMB data from WMAP and PLANCK, we understand a great deal about large scale structure formation. The MS program in Astronomy and Astrophysics focuses on underlying physics of phenomena beyond the Earth, theory, data analysis and modeling techniques, which will undoubtedly play a prominent role in international basic research. The key areas of focus include Gravitational Physics, Galaxy Evolution, Dynamics and Evolution of Supermassive Black Holes in Galaxy Centres, Modeling of Galaxy Merge

--- International Conference On Applications Of Space Science & Technology ---
The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST) marks a transformative milestone in Pakistan's journey through space science and technology. As the largest space event in the country, ICAST unites the legacies of IST’s International Conference on Aerospace Science and Engineering (ICASE) and SUPARCO’s International Conference on Space (ICS) into a biennial global forum of unparalleled excellence. ICAST bringing together visionaries, global thought leaders, pioneering researchers, and industry trailblazers, aspires to redefine the future of space science by fostering collaboration, presenting transformative research, and exploring interdisciplinary space applications with the potential to reshape our collective future.
International Conference On Applications Of Space Science & Technology

--- The International Conference on Engineering Advancements, Science and Technology (ICEAST) ---
The aim of the International Conference on Engineering Advancements, Science and Technology is to foster interdisciplinary collaboration among engineers, scientists, researchers, and industry professionals, providing a global platform for presenting cutting-edge research and technological innovations. Click on link to see details.
