--- BS Physics ---
--- Introduction ---
Physics investigates the laws of nature and is indispensable not only for our understanding of the world but also for the solution of the technological and ecological problems. As a fundamental science, physics continues to be the driving intellectual force in expanding our understanding of the universe, in discovering the scientific basis for new technologies, and in applying these technologies to research. The Institute of Space Technology has launched a new program for BS in Physics, starting from Fall 2020. The BS program in Physics is designed to enlighten students with knowledge from all the vast areas of Physics, including Classical Mechanics, Electrodynamics, Statistical Mechanics, Quantum Mechanics, Particle Physics, Plasma Physics, Condensed Matter Physics, Special and General Relativity etc. Alongside a sound basic education in Physics and Mathematics, BS students would be able to choose attractive, career-oriented study blocks, notably from the fields of Computational Physics, Materials Physics, Environmental Physics, Plasma Physics, High Energy Physics, Electronics and Astronomy & Astrophysics, which is a unique specialization of its kind in Pakistan. The Department of Space Science and Physics at IST has distinguished faculty to its credit, who engage in both quality teaching and research. The department's resources not only include the well-trained faculty, but also high-quality labs that provide excellent research opportunities for the students. Active research groups are studying the physics of elementary particles; theoretical and experimental investigations of plasmas ranging from ionosphere to controlled nuclear fusion; electric and magnetic properties of materials; semiconductor physics; astrophysical modeling of supermassive black hole pairs as sources of gravitational waves; observations of stars; study of gamma ray bursts. The students shall be introduced to the research world by working in the diverse fields under the su

--- MS - Global Navigation Satellite Systems ---
--- The Program ---
Institute of Space Technology, being the pioneer institute in space science, technology and applications reckoned the need of research and qualified human resource in the field of precise positioning, navigation and timing (PNT) and started a specialized Master of Science program in Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) in spring 2014. The program is structured according to the GNSS curriculum proposed and designed by the United Nations Office for Outer Space Affairs (UNOOSA) and the only program of its kind in Pakistan and the 4th across the globe after Italy, China and France. The MS GNSS course work covers eight subjects (24 credit hours) covering specific areas of GNSS (theory, technology and applications) followed by a six credit hours research thesis. Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) include constellations of Earth orbiting satellites in Medium Earth Orbits (MEO), networks of ground control and monitoring stations, and of receivers that calculate ground positions by the basic principle of trilateration. At present GNSS include two fully operational global navigation systems, the Global Positioning System (GPS) by US and the GLObal NAvigation Satellite System (GLONASS) by Russia. China has recently completed the launch of Beidou system while GALILEO by Europe is in the final phase of completion. In addition to global navigation systems, India has its own Regional Navigation Satellite System named as IRNSS / NavIC and Japan has the Quasi-Zenith Satellite System (QZSS). Once all these global and regional systems become fully operational, the user will have access to positioning, navigation and timing signals from more than 100 satellites. In addition to global and regional navigation satellite systems, there are also space based augmentation systems namely the Wide-area Augmentation System (WAAS) by US, the European Geostationary Navigation Overlay Service (EGNOS), the Russian System of Differential Correction and Monitoring (SD

--- MS Environment and Climate Sciences ---
--- Introduction ---
The advent of atmospheric sciences dates as far back as Aristotle who in his classical treatise, Meteorological laid some of the foundations of this expanding field. With the advent of ground instruments, computers and more recently observations from space, a new and exciting era of atmospheric and environmental sciences is unfolding. In the local context, Pakistan is heavily challenged due to extreme weather events and climate change including major massive flooding in the past four years. A major focus of the MS program is towards developing better understanding of these major issues and working towards providing solutions that are sustainable. The group at IST is focused on both physics and chemistry aspects of these issues and approaches complex environmental problems in a multidisciplinary and interdisciplinary manner. Further, both local and international collaborations are involved, which includes joint partnerships with Pakistan Meteorological Department and Environment Canada. The MS program in Atmospheric and Environmental Science thus focuses on underlying physics and chemistry of processes related to Earth which involve direct observation, data analysis and modeling approaches. Key focus areas include Atmospheric Physics and Chemistry, Cloud and Aerosol dynamics, Satellite and Ground Meteorology, Hydrology and Climate Change. Students in the MS program are trained at local and international standards for scientific scholarship and research. This is expected to open many arenas of professional opportunities towards meaningful contributions to their scientific learning.
--- Program Educational Objectives ---
PEO-1: Collaborate with Engineers and scientists from industry and academia as research/projects. PEO-2: Promote the culture of interdisciplinary novel research and produce fundamental & applied quality research in Pakistan PEO-3: Build environment and climate-oriented knowledge in the emerging areas of science and engineering, lik

--- PhD Astronomy and Astrophysics ---
--- Introduction ---
As the oldest of the natural sciences, astronomy and astrophysics is deeply intertwined with the evolution of human thinking, the development of new technologies as well as the understanding of natural phenomena. From the use of celestial bodies and stars for finding directions, to the understanding of the model of the Solar system, to the beginning of the space era and study of the nature of the Universe in the 20th century, astronomy and astrophysics has had a deep influence on human society. Currently, the field of Astronomy and Astrophysics is one of the most rapidly growing domains of study. This growth is made possible through the complimentary approaches of observation, computation/simulation and theory. Observational data are gathered through high performance telescopes in all domains of the electromagnetic spectrum as well as gravitational waves, neutrinos and charged particles. High performance computational systems allow the simulations of complex systems such as the collision of galaxies and the analysis of large amounts of data. All of this results in the refinement and development of better theoretical models of various objects and phenomena, ranging from the solar system, to stars and plasmas in the Milky Way, to the study of complex objects such as neutron stars, supernova explosions, galaxies and their supermassive black holes, and large scale structures in the Universe. IST’s Astronomy and Astrophysics PhD program aims to train future researchers through quality education in astronomy and astrophysics. The courses are geared towards fostering the investigative capacity, creative thinking and intellectual curiosity of students. While the research projects hone out the multiple skills required by students in order to finish a successful PhD dissertation and carry out their own research endeavours in the future.
--- Research Areas ---
Currently research is carried out in several domains of astronomy and astrophysics. The Astronomy

--- BS Biotechnology ---
--- Intro ---
The program is offered as a full-time course with a duration of four years, extendable to six years according to IST's statues and HEC standards and rules; and leads, at the end of the program, to a BS degree in Biotechnology. The total credit hours are 132 and all students follow the same core courses; however, they may opt for different elective courses. The students who do not complete the program requirements are not awarded any degree; but they are provided with a transcript on their request. A student is said to have a good standing if his/her CGPA is 2.00 or more. However, student is cautioned if CGPA is 2.00 or above and SGPA falls below 2.00. A student is given the status of probation (Temporary Enrolment) if CGPA falls below 2.00, and if during probation his/her CGPA falls below 2.00 and SGPA is 2.00 or more then the student gets a second probation (Extended Temporary Enrolment). Student is dropped out if his/her SGPA is below 2.00 during probation (Temporary Enrolment) or his/her CGPA is below 2.00 during second probation (Extended Temporary Enrolment). The courses are categorized into four domains: general education cluster, minor, interdisciplinary, and major courses. In addition to passing all the courses with the required CGPA, the student has to attain all twelve PLOs, to earn a BS (Biotechnology) degree.
--- Program Mission ---
"The Biotechnology program endeavors to foster future leaders in research, academia, and industry with a specialized undergraduate curriculum. Our focus on cutting-edge research aligns with the evolving needs of society, integrating principles that resonate with space technology, thereby advancing scientific exploration, entrepreneurship, and societal well-being."

--- BS Artificial Intelligence ---
--- Introduction ---

IST has done the necessary homework to make it a very competitive program. The program of computer science will allow the students to involve themselves in the solution development of the real-world problems and open the opportunities for the students as potential candidates for private or public sector organization, freelance developers and entrepreneurs.

--- Program Educational Objectives ---
PEO-01: Apply mechanical engineering knowledge to identify and address technological and societal problems. PEO-02: Enhance intellectual and analytical abilities to take initiative and/or develop innovative ideas for technological and professional growth in mechanical and allied disciplines. PEO-03: Work effectively and ethically as a team member/leader in a multidisciplinary environment while demonstrating interpersonal and managerial skills.
Program Educational Objectives

--- Program Learning Outcomes ---
The Department of Mechanical Engineering has adopted the Program Learning Outcomes defined by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) and are supported by our defined PEOs. These PLOs relate to the aptitude, awareness and performance that students acquire with the progression of the program. PLO-01: Engineering Knowledge: An ability to apply knowledge of mathematics, science and engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PLO-02: Problem Analysis: An ability to identify, formulate, research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences and engineering sciences. PLO-03: Design/Development of Solutions: An ability to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design systems, components, or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health and safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PLO-04: Investigation: An ability to investigate complex engineering problems in a methodical way including literature survey, design and conduct of experiments, analysis and interpretation of experimental data, and synthesis of information to derive valid conclusions. PLO-05: Modern Tool Usage: An ability to create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modeling, to complex engineering activities, with an understanding of the limitations. PLO-06: The Engineer and Society: An ability to apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice and solution to complex engineering problems. PLO-07: Environment and Sustainability: An ability to understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate knowledg

--- MS in Fluid & Thermal Systems ---
--- Introduction ---
Fluid and Thermal System (FTS) program aims to impart the understanding of intensely practical, design-oriented engineering problems pertaining to the technical challenges of the country facing nowadays. We aim to grow and evoke the rational skills that will help in solving the multi-disciplinary engineering problems. This program focuses on various advanced courses such as thermal system design, combustion, fluid mechanics, heat transfer, and computational fluid dynamics to prepare the graduate to create innovative new products and solutions to real world problems. This field of specialization is diverse which involves experimental, analytical and numerical investigations in the following areas: Computational Fluid Dynamics HVAC and Refrigeration Thermal Design of Heat Exchanger Thermal Systems Design Fluid Structure Interactions Combustion Turbomachinery This program enables the graduates apply their knowledge and skills to develop the effective, efficient and environmental friendly systems.

--- MS in Mechanical Design & Analysis ---
--- Introduction ---
Mechanical Design and Analysis (MDA) program focus to develop the core mechanical engineering skills related to the design, analysis and synthesis of the mechanical parts/systems/devices. These skills are considered as the core of any product design & development industry for a range of exciting careers in the field of mechanical engineering. Moreover, this program enables the graduates to contribute in the research and development of solution for advance mechanical engineering problems. Key research areas for this field of specialization are: Linear and Nonlinear Behavior of Mechanical Structures Modeling and Simulation of Mechanical Systems Product Design and Analysis Vibrations, Chaos, and Machine Condition Monitoring etc. MDA is a most valuable and pragmatic program that develop a strong technical, analytical, and problem solving skills essential for a range of exciting careers in the challenging field of perfunctory engineering. This program enables the graduate’s engineers to contribute in the development of new and better solutions for mechanical components & systems, production equipment and industrial plants.

--- MS in Manufacturing Systems ---
--- Introduction ---
Manufacturing being one of the crucial aspect of Mechanical Engineering has a key role in uplifting the nation's production capacity, economy, and national exports. Therefore, this institute has a very high responsibility to train its graduates in the area of design and manufacturing to meet the requirements of the local industry in line with the modern techniques. This program focuses on the area of Computer-Aided Manufacturing, knowledge about modern techniques and advanced processes widely in the industry, rapid manufacturing, tooling, fabrication methods, advanced manufacturing systems using IoT, and smart factory concepts. Key areas for research include the following: Advanced Manufacturing Technologies and Systems Product Design and Development Additive Manufacturing Integrated Manufacturing Operations Management Laser Material Processing Design For Manufacturing and Assembly This program will prepare the graduates to apply knowledge of manufacturing engineering from the stages of the product design to manufacturing operations for an effective system integration.

--- MS in Automobile ---
--- Introduction ---
Specialization in Automobiles concerned with the support of life cycle design, manufacturing, durability testing and performance of vehicles. The rapid devolopement in the modren and innovative automotive technology, young engineers broaden a complete understanding of the complex field of Automobiles. The future engineers of the automobile can select one field of study out of more in the following areas: Vehicle dynamics & vibrations Fuel and emission systems Chassis and power train systems Crash safety Design, manufacturing and assembling This specialization provides hands-on education based on the latest scientific results and techniques, that enables the young engineers to work independently and with confidence. Moreover, graduate students enhance social and scientific skills. The program also enables them to face ever-increasing challenges and standards of international markets.

--- MS in Dynamics and Control ---
--- Introduction ---
Dynamics and Control aims to introduce the concepts of mathematical modelling, simulation, underlying forces and control theory to measure the performance of the multifaceted engineering systems. Further, it provides to examine or improve the dynamic response with the aid of control strategies for stabilizing the complex and unstable systems. Dynamics and Control is primarily focused by combining a variety of interesting fields such as nonlinear dynamics, mechatronics, robotics, dynamics of plates and shells, rotor-dynamics, control of mechanical systems, and acoustics etc. Specific areas of interest include: Nonlinear Dynamics of Physical Systems Application of Control Theory to Autonomous/Distributed Systems Space Systems and Vibrations Vibro and Flow Acoustics Students will acquire an exposure to analyze the real dynamic systems, identify or evaluate the system parameters, stability requirements, and the control schemes to regulate them. In nutshell, Dynamics and Control is a multi-disciplinary area in which students will be able to model and solve the dynamical complex systems in a variety of disciplines such as mechanical, aerospace, electrical, avionics, biological and so forth.

--- Program Educational Objective ---
Enhance competency in contributing towards scientific/engineering knowledge and generate new opportunities for the advancement of society. Apply analytical, intellectual and methodological skills to conduct independent and original research in their relevant area. Interact with people from various backgrounds productively, professionally and also follow the ethical standards in their field of study. Publish and present their research work with confidence and receive grants by writing proposals.
Program Educational Objective

--- Program Learning Outcomes ---
Advancement of Knowledge: To practice mechanical engineering in their particular field of study for the advancement of knowledge. Problem Solving: To apply in-depth knowledge of their chosen field in mechanical engineering using interdisciplinary approach for problem solving. Research and Development: To adapt, create, transfer and assimilate current and emerging technologies into new products through relevant research. Society and Environment: To understand the societal and environmental constraints in context of professional engineering solutions. Communication: To communicate ideas correctly and effectively. Ethics: To understand the social and ethical responsibilities. Lifelong Learning: Pursue lifelong learning to improve their knowledge and skills continuously in the broader context of innovation and technological developments.
Program Learning Outcomes

--- Mechanical Design & Analysis ---

--- Program Educational Objectives ---
After graduation, our students will be able to: Plan, analyze and investigate engineering problems using contemporary design and simulation tools. Promote the culture of interdisciplinary research among the engineering disciplines and applied mathematics Explore the new emerging areas of science and engineering like Nonlinear Dynamics, Computational Mathematics and Cryptography Sense the needs of their profession thus they will become cognizant of ethics, social responsibilities and capable enough to demonstrate knowledge for sustainable development Communicate effectively and will work meritoriously as a team member and lead multidisciplinary teams.
Program Educational Objectives

--- Program Learning Outcomes ---
The curriculum for MS/PhD students is designed by the collaboration of mathematicians and engineers. Students undertaking research in this department will have a chance to learn not only the fundamental courses of engineering but also advanced courses related to their area of specialization and interest. Emerging specializations in the domain of engineering, like Nonlinear Dynamics, Computational mathematics and Cryptography, will be offered as area of research for graduate students at this department. As AM&S is also working as supporting department for other engineering and sciences department so upon successful completion of the courses taught by mathematics faculty, students will be able to: Apply knowledge of mathematics, science and engineering fundamentals to the solution of complex problems involved in different engineering areas. Identify, formulate, research literature and analyze mathematical models governing laws of physics and other engineering sciences. Design solution strategy for mathematical models arising in aerospace engineering, electrical engineering, Mechanical engineering and other in science and engineering disciplines. Conduct investigation into complex systems of differential equations using research based knowledge and research methods including analysis and interpretation of data and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusion. Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modeling the physical phenomena with an understanding of the limitations. Apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to access societal, health, safety, legal, cultural and economic issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to their professions. Understand impact of mathematical solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate knowledge and need for sustainable development. Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and

--- BS - Mathematics ---
--- Introduction ---
The department offers the degree of Bachelor of Science (BS) in Mathematics as a major with followings minors Data Science Artificial Intelligence Thus, students will have options to earn degrees in BS-Mathematics, BS-Mathematics with a focus on Data Science (Single Major-Single Minor) and BS Mathematics with a focus on Artificial Intelligence (Single Major-Single Minor). The streams with “Single Major-Single Minor” are per guidelines of new undergraduate policy of the Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan.
--- Program Mission Statement ---
The mission of the program is to provide students with a platform in mathematics that strengthens the foundation of theoretical, applied mathematics, and edge-cutting technologies in students to make them highly marketable regarding their future endeavors.
--- Program Educational Objectives ---
After graduation, our students will be equipped not only with advanced mathematical tools but will also acquire skill set needed to apply mathematics towards engineering problems. Moreover, our graduates will be able to: PEO-01: Apply mathematical and related knowledge to identify and address the technical and societal problems. PEO-02: Enhance their intellectual and analytical abilities in taking initiatives and/or develop innovative ideas for professional growth in mathematics and allied disciplines. PEO-03: Work effectively as a team member or lead multidisciplinary teams while demonstrating the interpersonal & management skills and ethical responsibilities. PEO-04: Pursue professional career in education, business & finance, industry and research institutions OR continue higher education to obtain advanced degrees in mathematics or related fields.

--- PhD Mathematics ---
--- Introduction ---
The department of Applied Mathematics & Statistics is offering PhD programs. The fields of specialization for PhD programs are given below: Nonlinear Dynamics Fluid Mechanics/Computational Fluid Dynamics Computational Mathematics Algebra (Group Theory and Generalization, Algebraic Geometry) Cryptography Statistics (Applied Statistics, Bayesian and Monte Carlo Statistics, Sampling Techniques, Quality Control)
--- Program Mission Statement ---
The mission of PhD program is to produce innovative leaders in the field of mathematics who will be able to integrate the roles of researchers, educators and intellectual leaders by conducting original, relevant and rigorous research in the field of mathematics in accordance with national and international demands.
--- Program Educational Objectives ---
PhD students, after graduating, will be able to PEO-1: serve highly consequential functions in society, including the fields of teaching and social impact-based research, in view of national and international demands. PEO-2: become effective members of computing related organizations for the design of algorithms and their implementations in computational sciences and applied sciences related to modern industry. PEO-3: become effective members of national and international R & D organizations working in interdisciplinary fields of mathematics and engineering where they can formulate, analyze, evaluate, validate, and extract scientific conclusions. PEO-4: enhance their critical thinking and mathematical skills, holding ethical values, such that they can analyze data and interpret it for revealing the facts about physical happenings and contribute to the community as a responsible person.
--- Program Structure ---
Course Work Minimum 18 credit hours of course work must be undertaken with at least CGPA of 3.0 out of band 4.0. Migration/Transfer of the courses from other Universities/Institutes is allowed as per IST Migration/Transfer policy The list of six PhD co

--- MS - Mathematics ---
--- Introduction ---
The department of Applied Mathematics & Statistics is offering MS and PhD programs. The fields of specialization for MS/PhD programs are given below: Nonlinear Dynamics Fluid Mechanics/Computational Fluid Dynamics Computational Mathematics Cryptography Algebra (Group Theory and Generalization, Algebraic Geometry) Statistics (Applied Statistics, Bayesian and Monte Carlo Statistics, Sampling Techniques, Quality Control)
--- Program Mission Statement ---
The mission of the MS-Mathematics degree program in the Department of Applied Mathematics & Statistics is to prepare graduate manpower to pursue advanced degrees or serve at entry-level employment in interdisciplinary fields related to mathematics, engineering and academia to cater to national demands.
--- Program Educational Objectives ---
The MS-graduates should be able to PEO-1: apply knowledge of mathematics and related skills in carrying out original research in mathematics and interdisciplinary fields. PEO-2: contribute to the community as a responsible member with ethical values in their individual capacities as well as in team work . PEO-3: engage themselves in activities that help them in pursuit of learning, self-improvement, and enhancement of professional and intellectual abilities in their specialized areas.

Humanities & Sciences

--- Bio Materials Lab ---
We offer a wide variety of bio-nanofabrication and materials characterization facilities available in the Biomaterials Lab (IST, Islamabad) for students, faculty, and professionals outside of IST. Our services include the following: Additive Manufacturing (3D Printing and Electrospinning) Electrophoretic Deposition (EPD) Fourier Transform Infrared Spectroscopy (FTIR) Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area analyzer (BET) Nanoindentation UV-Vis Spectroscopy Adhesion Testing of coatings Wettability (Water/Ethanol/Body Fluid Contact Angle Measurement) Profilometry (Surface Roughness) Scratch tester Corrosion and Wear tests Biological characterization (Antibacterial Tests, CAM Assay, Bioactivity, Biodegradation, Drug Release, Protein Adsorption, etc.) Room temperature and subzero (-80°C) storage facilities Incubators and Centrifuges Other facilities, including SEM/EDX, XRD, AFM, Hardness testing, Tensile test, Particle size analysis, Furnaces, etc., are also available at the Department of Materials Science and Engineering, IST.
Bio Materials Lab

International Webinars (With IEEE ASP) 40th Digital Avionics System Conference, San Antonio Tx, USA; AESS Avionics Systems Panel Research and Education Perspectives 41st Digital Avionics System Conference, Portsmouth VA, USA; Application & Certification Concerns for Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning Techniques in Safety Critical Avionics Systems 42nd Digital Avionics System Conference, Barcelona, Spain; Intelligent Cyber Physical Systems for Integrated Air & Space Operations

--- Pre-Incubation Program ---
In recent years, the world has observed a revolutionary shift from job seekers to job creators. Business Incubation Center at IST working with the aim to encourage entrepreneurial culture and mindset among IST students, faculty, alumni and general public. After having many interventions for IST students, BIC has launched a Pre-Incubation Program to encourage and facilitate IST students to create their own Technology based startup to become the job creators rather a job seeker. Furthermore, the program focus would be to provide an opportunity to IST students to commercialize their innovative ideas. Selected startups under Pre-incubation program would have access to all support and facilitation of IST Business Incubation Center and a desk space in a Co-working space dedicated for Pre-Incubation at IST BIC. BIC at IST provide range of facilities for IST student: Internet access Co-working office space Utility services Access to lab and equipment Access to funds Legal and business consultancy Well educated workforce Advance computer labs Our mentors and business consultants are those who are devoted and dedicated to accelerate the growth of startups. They are Encouraging IST students to think innovatively and enable them to perform efficiently in such a competitive market. Registration: 
Pre-Incubation Program

--- Admissions ---
Introduction The admissions for BSCS are held twice a year in Spring and Fall Semesters. The admissions for BSCE are held once a year in Fall Semester. The maximum number of seats available for admissions in BSCS is 100 per year, BSCE program is 40 per year. The admissions for MSCS are held twice a year in Spring and Fall Semesters. FEE SUBMISSION You can pay fee in any branch of Askari Commercial Bank or by easypaisa with following details. KICSIT Account No 01761650500136 Account Title: KICSIT -KRL Kahuta Application Processing Fee Rs.2000/-
Apply Online

--- Financial Aid & Scholarships ---
Scholarships for BSCS and BSCE Every year KICSIT provides scholarships exceeding worth Rs. 2.0 Millions to the talented and needy students. The number of scholarships KICSIT provides, exceeds the percentage requirement of scholarships set by HEC. . Merit Award for Students (1st To 8th Semesters) Merit Award is given on the basis of excellent academic performance to Students from 1st To 8th Semesters. The top three position-holders in each semester are given this award worth 50%, 37.5%, & 25% of tuition fee (of KRL Wards) waivers respectively. To qualify for this award students should have earned SGPA of 3.0 or above for semester II to VII and 70% marks or above in Intermediate result for first semester. In case of equal SGPAs, positions are given on the basis of higher percentage. This award is not dependent on any other scholarship. Need Based Scholarships KICSIT provides financial support to talented needy students in the form KICSIT Need Based Scholarships. This scholarship is open for all onboard students and they can be from any fee category. The applicant should have obtained SGPA of 3.00 or above (70% or above in FSc for first year students).The applicant should submit application form along with all relevant documents. at the start of the semester after final result notification from KICSIT CoE office. Scholarships will be awarded for only one semester but can be re-availed in subsequent semesters, if the need prevails and for this student will have to put up fresh application. A student can avail only one scholarship at a time except Merit Award. Decision for the award of scholarships and amounts will be made on case to case basis as recommended by Scholarship Committee and approved by Director General KICSIT. HEC Ehsas Undergraduate Scholarship The Ehsas Undergraduate Scholarship Project focuses on the most critical segment, namely the 4-5year undergraduate programs, which contributes the most to income of the individual who completes the bachelor’s degree

[TOPIC: Campus Life Section]
Campus Life Section

Student Experience
Campus Life
Building a vibrant community of creative and accomplished people from around the world
Facilities at IST
Vibrant student life fostering growth and leadership
Overview Going to IST is about making the most of your life as a student. As you get involved, you'll not only have a lot of fun, you'll also develop your leadership abilities, raise your consciousness, and acquire skills you'll use after graduation. Together students, faculty and staff of IST create a welcoming and enterprising community to achieve all this. List of student societies is always evolving with the changing interests of students. Are you interested in academics, athletics, socializing, social work, performing arts, gaming or movies? There's a good chance that a student society exists for what you want to do. Wellness and Counseling Center Wellness and Counseling Center, IST is dedicated to help students, faculty and staff members maximize their sense of overall well-being and facilitate them towards making healthy contributions to their academic, personal, social and career development. The Centre provides free and confidential on- campus psychological counseling services, both in individual or group settings, where the students address their concerns with a highly trained mental health clinician in a safe and non-judgmental environment. The Centre further aims to highlight social stigma and spread awareness regarding various topics of mental health through workshops, advocacy and outreach programs. For your convenience, there are many ways to book a counseling appointment for your professional and personal concerns with either a male or a female therapist, You can email our psychologists directly at annum.tanweer@ist.edu.pk or taj.iqbal@ist.edu.pk You can call directly at 051-9075859 between 9 a.m to 12 p.m - Monday to Friday. You can visit Department of Humanities and Sciences, IST or Wellness and Counseling Center at Second Floor, Rashid Block, IST. Housing & Dining Hostel Registration Form Boarding Facility Boarding facilities will be limited to students living outside Rawalpindi / Islamabad area. IST is managing 03 x hostels for boys and 1 x hostel for girls. Students are expected to bring decent colour linen and their amenities with them at the time of joining. Convocation 2025
A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements!
A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements! The atmosphere was electric today as we congratulated the brilliant graduates of the 18th Convocation of IST . Your hard work, dedication, and perseverance have finally culminated in this magnificent success. Today, we didn't just award degrees; we launched future leaders, innovators, and change-makers. Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics"
Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics.” The session went beyond theoretical discussion, focusing on how language subtly shapes everyday experiences. It highlighted the ways in which word choices, silences, and patterns of speech reflect underlying structures of power, identity, and gender often in ways that remain unnoticed. The event was particularly enriched by the active engagement of the participants. Their insightful questions, personal reflections, and willingness to reconsider familiar linguistic practices through new perspectives made the session especially meaningful. Such interactions reaffirm Dr. Ausima’s continued commitment to research, teaching, and critical dialogue in the field of sociolinguistics.

Ms. Qurat-ul-Ain and Ms. Ifrah Jamil , lecturers Humanities and Sciences Department, attended the Pakistan English Conference titled “Rethinking English Studies in the Global South”, at Government College University, Lahore on November 27, 2025. [TOPIC: Vision & Mission]
Vision & Mission

--- Vision ---
Be a National torchbearer, in the realm of academics, through quality teaching, robust research and outreach – to produce leaders in the field of Space Science and Technology, in line with National aspirations.
--- Mission ---
To provide a conducive environment for realization of the full potential of faculty and students. To provide a conducive environment for realization of the full potential of faculty and students. To produce competent, balanced and creative graduates capable of assuming leadership positions to tackle future national/international technological challenges. To create awareness of and interest in Space Education at all societal tiers for sustainable national development.

[TOPIC: Harassment Complaint Cell]
Harassment Complaint Cell

The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is committed to fostering an all-inclusive and supportive learning environment for its community. In abidance with The Higher Education Commission’s (HEC) Policy on Protection against Sexual Harassment in Higher Education Institutions , IST has a zero-tolerance policy for any untoward conduct that violates the dignity of any individual within the institution. The Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC) was established on 20 February 2012, for monitoring and protection against sexual harassment of any individual at IST. It has since developed policies and procedures to address sexual and gender-based harassment so that members of the IST community feel safe and secure in a harassment- free environment since they have an avenue to voice their grievances. For further information about the policies or harassment issues, kindly contact: Dr. Rahila Naz, Head of the Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC), IST Room number 212, Block 2, IST 051-9075562 head.hcc@ist.edu.pk Every member of the IST community has the right to work and conduct learning, research, and scholarship without being subject to any form of discrimination, harassment, or social pressure. The HCC’s investigative process is designed to be confidential and fair. No reprimand will be initiated for filing a legitimate complaint. HCC will ensure the protection of the rights of the complainant and the accused. Eliminating harassment from educational institutions requires a cohesive effort by its community – the HCC is IST’s spearhead in this endeavor.
--- Harassment Policy ---
Document: HEC Policy Guidelines on Harassment (2023-07-19)
Document: Protection Against Harassment of Women at the Workplace Act, 2010 (2023-06-19)
Harassment Complaint Cell

[TOPIC: Visiting IST]
Visiting IST

Away from congestion, noise and pollution of the city, at approx. 20 minutes drive from Islamabad and Rawalpindi, having multiple access through Islamabad Highway and GT Road, IST is located in the Capital Territory of Pakistan. This advantageous location offers round the clock accessibility through public and private transportation.
--- From Rawalpindi/Islamabad through Faizabad ---
From Faizabad it is about 13 Mins, 19.7 KM drive Through Faizabad Interchange , enter into Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) and continue driving towards Islamabad Airport Continue onto Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) pass through Khanna Bridge ( about 4.6 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) pass through Koral Flyover (Old Air Port Chok) and enter onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) ( about 3.06 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) pass through Kaak Pul (Suan River Bridge) ( about 8.1 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) the road will slightly curve right and you will find Canyon Views-EMAAR ( on the left 2.2 KM ), keep in right lane for the U-turn ahead Immediately after Canyon Views-EMAAR take the U-turn just before the Islamabad Toll Plaza( about 250 meters ) After the U-turn take the first left turn out from Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) ( about 300 meters ) Stop for security check and then turn right, Parking is straight ahead and IST is on the Left
--- From Saddar, Rawalpindi, through Katchehri Chowk ---
From Katchehri Chowk it is about 15.2 km, 15 mins drive From Katchehri Chowk , continue onto G.T. Road towards Rawat Drive for about 7.9 KMs and take a Slight right onto Sawan River Bridge and continue onto G.T. Road After about 5.8 KMs Turn left onto Islamabad Highway Pass through Islamabad Toll Plaza ( about 360 meters ) After toll plaza take the first left turn out from Islamabad Highway ( about 380 meters ) Stop for security check and then turn right, Parking is straight ahead and IST is on the Left
Visiting IST

[TOPIC: Why IST]
Why IST

Space is considered to be the final frontier and world’s best minds take up the most daunting challenges to conquer it. Fazeel Mahmood Khan, PhD The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is a premier institution in Pakistan, renowned for its focus on space and related sciences. Here's why IST stands out: Why Choose IST? 1. Specialized Education: IST offers specialized education in space and related sciences, equipping students to address national and global issues. 2. Research Opportunities: The institute has state-of-the-art research labs, providing students with hands-on experience and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research. 3. Accreditation and Recognition: IST is federally chartered, HEC recognized, and PEC accredited, ensuring its degrees are valued globally. 4. Industry Connections: IST has strong industry connections, facilitating internships, placements, and job opportunities for its graduates. 5. Student Life: The institute offers a vibrant student life, with various societies, clubs, and sports activities, fostering personal growth and development. 6. Scholarships and Sponsorships: IST offers merit-based scholarships and sponsorships, making quality education accessible to deserving students. 7. Global Recognition: IST's programs are accredited under Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), substantially equivalent to Washington Accord, enabling graduates to work globally. Unique Achievements 1. Satellite Development: IST has successfully developed and launched satellites, including iCUBE1 and ICUBE-Qamar, demonstrating its expertise in space technology. 2. Research and Innovation: The institute is committed to research and innovation, with a strong focus on solving real-world problems. Career Opportunities At the IST, we empower students to pursue their passion for space science and technology. Since 2002, we've been producing professionals who meet the demands of national organizations and contribute to the nation's technological advancements. Thriving Careers Ahead Our graduates enjoy excellent career prospects, both locally and globally. They're employed in leading organizations, including: • Space agencies (SUPARCO, European Space Agencies) • Aviation industry (Airbus, Emirates, Air Blue) • Research institutes (R&D, academia) • Government organizations (PTA, WAPDA, EPA) • Private sector (mobile communication, agriculture, airlines) Diverse Employment Opportunities Our multidisciplinary approach opens doors to vari

[TOPIC: Admission FAQ]
Admission FAQ

Q: What is the application process?
A: The application process involves filling out an online application form, uploading required documents, and paying the application processing fee. Online admission module can be accessed by clicking the link IST Admissions
Q: How many seats are for each program?
A: Engineering Programs: Aerospace Engineering : 100 - Avionics Engineering : 80 – Electrical Engineering : 100 – Mechanical Engineering : 120 – Metallurgy & Materials Engineering : 80 Computing Programs: Computer Science : 100 – Artificial Intelligence : 50 – Data Science : 50 – Software Engineering: 50 – Computer Engineering: 50 Science Programs : Space Science : 50 – Physics: 40 – Math: 50 – RS&GIS: 50 – Chemistry (Nanotechnology): 50 – Biotechnology: 50
Q: What is duration of study for each Program?
A: The study duration of each program is 04 years (08 semesters).
Q: How many times admissions are announced for Engineering, Non-Engineering & Computing Programs in a year?
A: Admissions for BS programs are announced once in a year i.e. in March/ April.
Q: Does an applicant can apply for all engineering, computing & science programs?
A: Yes.
Q: What is the eligibility criteria, merit determination criteria & fee structure for BS programs?
A: Updated information is available and can be accessed by using the below links:   
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS engineering programs?
A: IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. Please use the below link for details: 
Q: I have already appeared for entry test but I do not receive result card. What should I do?
A: Please get a print of your result card from website of testing authority and upload with online application form
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS computing & non-engineering programs?
Q: Does the institute have affiliation with any university/ institution?
A: IST is a federally chartered, HEC recognized and PEC accredited, degree awarding university therefore it does not require affiliation with any other university/ institution. Please use the below link for details: 
Q: Does Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) recognize the Institute?
A: IST has been awarded accreditation for its various undergraduate engineering programs at Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), making them fully compliant with the Washington Accord. Engineering graduates obtaining a degree under this category of HEIs are considered at par with world class professionals in the field, and do

[TOPIC: Foreign Applicant]
Foreign Applicant

--- Al Khwarizmi Scholarships for Foreign Students ---
Scholarships are available for Foreign Students to study in Bachelor/ Master/ Doctoral Degree Programs offered by IST. Duration of Scholarship: For BS (Bachelor’s) Students: Upto 04 years For MS (Master’s) Students: Upto 2.5 years For PhD (Doctoral) Students: Upto 04 years The scholarship program covers funds for the following expenses: University tuition fee as per actual Stipend @ PKR 15000, 20000 & 35000 per month for students admitted in BS, MS & PhD programs respectively Stipend @ PKR 50000 per month for scientist/ post doc fellow One time return air ticket Mandatory academic & non-academic charges like admission fee, endowment fund, library development fund, service charges, safe/ smart campus charges, hostel charges etc. are not covered.
--- Admission of Foreign Applicants in IST ---
--- Admission Pre-requisites ---
Candidates willing to apply for admission in BS, MS/ PhD programs offered by IST must: meet the eligibility requirements ( BS , MS , PhD ) for respective program as mentioned on IST website be able/ willing to pay the semester dues/ allied charges as per fee structure ( BS , MS, PhD ) given on IST website for foreign Students. All fees and other charges are subject to revision from time to time be proficient in English as all educational and extracurricular activities such as "Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc" at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC / HEC , Pakistan (keeping in view the eligibility requirements to apply for respective program) before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST. In case any document proves to be false, fake, or fabricated at a later stage, a provisionally admitted student shall be liable to expulsion from the University at any stage or to any other disciplinary or legal action the University may deem necessary obtain No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their Ministry of Foreign Affairs or their Embassy in Pakistan prepare personal statement, in English, should include self-introduction, study and work experience, reasons for application and study plan/ research proposal obtain two letters of recommendation from full professors or associate professors, in English. (Professors’ signature, contact phone number and email address must be put on the letter) be willi

[TOPIC: Financial Aid & Scholarships]
Financial Aid & Scholarships

--- Sponsorship for BS Students ---
Several elite National Research & Development (R&D) organizations provide sponsorships to the deserving BS students of selected programs after test/interview. The sponsorship covers tuition fee and a guaranteed job after graduation, provided all requirements of the sponsoring organization are fulfilled by the candidate.
--- Merit Scholarships for BS Students ---
IST offers merit scholarships to three students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per the existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of admissions merit and semester results. Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to BS students of each batch of each undergraduate program offered by IST:-
Table 1:
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: Ser. | col_1: Scholarship Type | col_2: Merit Position | col_3: Tuition Fee Waiver | col_4: Minimum Criteria
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 01 | col_1: IST-1 | col_2: 1st | col_3: 100 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 02 | col_1: IST-2 | col_2: 2nd | col_3: 50 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 03 | col_1: IST-3 | col_2: 3rd | col_3: 25 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: FOR SUBSEQUENT SEMESTERS:
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: Ser. | col_1: Scholarship Type | col_2: Merit Position | col_3: Tuition Fee Waiver | col_4: Minimum Criteria
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 01 | col_1: IST-1 | col_2: 1st | col_3: 100 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 02 | col_1: IST-2 | col_2: 2nd | col_3: 50 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 03 | col_1: IST-3 | col_2: 3rd | col_3: 25 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
GENERAL CONDITIONS: Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship. Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. However, both SGPA and CGPA will be considered to ascertain the eligibility of the student for Scholarship. Students getting any type of scholarships/ sponsored by any organization are not eligible for merit scholarship. Student will be awarded only one scholarship (merit or need based) from IST’s own funds (whichever is higher) at a time. In case of two or more students securing the same merit position (Admissions’ Merit Position/ same SGPA and CGPA), allocated amounts of respective scholarships for these positions shall be equally distributed among them. If students ‘A’ & ‘B’ jointly declared at 1st position, then the student ‘C’

--- Flow Chart ---
--- Steps of Enrollment in BS Programs offered by IST ---
Step 1 - Admissions Announcement on IST Website/ National Newspapers Undergraduate (BS) admissions are announced on IST Website/ National Newspapers once a year i.e. in March/April. Step 2 - Completion of Online Admission Form Candidates interested to apply for admission in IST are required to visit www.ist.edu.pk or  to create an online account, read & understand the eligibility & merit determination criteria, fee structure, fee refund policy and other related information. Subsequently, eligible candidates willing to apply for admission in IST are required to complete online admission form by providing desired information and uploading the scan copies of required documents with online admission form. Step 3 - Deposit of Application Processing Fee After creating an online account, applicants are required to download the challan form available at online admission form to deposit of application processing fee (non-refundable) in any online branch of Meezan Bank/HBL (within due date). It is a mandatory requirement. An email is sent for the confirmation of receipt of application processing fee and in parallel; fee receipt status is also updated at online admission form. In case if the application processing fee status is not updated at online admission within one week after the deposit of application processing fee then such applicants are required to contact Admissions Office with proof through email. Step 4 - Merit Lists/ Admissions Status Applicants are required to check their admission status on IST website by using the login details. An email/ SMS is also sent to provisionally selected applicants. However, applicants are strongly advised to keep visiting the IST website for updated information. Step 5 – Acceptance of Admission Provisionally selected applicants in respective programs are required to download the admission package by accessing the online admission system preferably through IST website. Admission package contain the offer letter, fee challan form/s, an affidavit (write-up), fee refund policy and other documents required to be submitted for registration in IST. Step 6 – Reservat

[TOPIC: Entry Test]
Entry Test

--- Entry Test for Engineering Programs – Fall 2026 Intake ---
IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. The results of following entry tests are acceptable to apply for admission in undergraduate programs offered by IST. Applicants can appear in any one of the below mentioned tests and can use the higher score to apply for admission in IST. Please note that an applicant must have minimum 33% obtained marks in entry test to apply for admission in IST: NAT which is conducted by NTS in every month HAT which is conducted by ETC-HEC NET which is conducted by NUST Islamabad ECAT which is conducted by UET Lahore ETEA which is conducted by UET Peshawar Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by MUET Jamshoro Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by NED Karachi ACT which is conducted by ACT-USA Further details related to acceptable entry tests dates & categories are as under: NAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories The test is conducted by NTS in all major cities of Pakistan in every month. Interested students are required to visit NTS website i.e.  or contact NTS to get desired information related to the registration procedure, test dates and centers etc. Acceptable Dates: The results of the NAT conducted by NTS from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: NAT-1E and NAT-1CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs HAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories HEC has established Education Testing Commission (ETC) to hold Higher Education Aptitude Test (HAT) for UG admissions. Acceptable Dates: The results of the HAT-UG tests announced/ available from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: HAT–UG–E and HAT–UG–CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs NET Acceptable Test Dates & Categories The result of NUST Entry Test (NET) announced/ available from December, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: NET (Engineering Category), NET (Computer Science) both are acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs ECAT Acceptable Test Date & Category The result of ECAT conducted by University of Engineering & Technology (UET) Lahore for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IS

[TOPIC: Merit Determination Criteria - MS]
Merit Determination Criteria - MS

Admission shall be granted absolutely on merit which will be determined on the basis of marks obtained in the following examinations, and according to theweightagementioned against the respective examination: For MSc (02 years) Students*: SSC/equivalent: 10% FSc/equivalent: 10% BSc/equivalent (02 years): 20% MSc/equivalent (02 years): 20% GAT General/HAT General/GRE International (General): 40% * These applicants can apply for non-engineering programs only For BSc/BS/BE (04 years) Students: SSC/equivalent: 10% FSc/equivalent: 10% BSc/BS/BE/ equivalent (04 years): 40% GAT General/HAT General/GRE International (General): 40%
--- Notes: ---
Only those MS & PhD candidates who have passed the BS/ MS/ M.Phil./ equivalent examination shall be eligible for admission to the next higher class. The result of pre-requisite degree/certificate for admission in any discipline should be officially notified by the respective body/ authority of the university till the date of closing of admission. It is mandatory for MS/ PhD applicants to pass the GAT/ GRE/ HAT in the relevant field of study with required marks/ score to apply for admission in IST. However, provisional admission without GAT/ GRE/ HAT may be granted only for first semester subject to availability of seats. Applicants with a foreign qualification are required to obtain the equivalent certificate from HEC before applying for admission in MS/ PhD programs offered by IST. It is mandatory for Government employees to submit NOC from their organization on registration day If a sufficient number of students are available only then Program/ Specialization will be started.
Merit Determination Criteria - MS

[TOPIC: Merit Determination Criteria - PhD]
Merit Determination Criteria - PhD

Admission shall be granted absolutely on merit which will be determined on the basis of marks obtained in the following examinations, and according to the weightage mentioned against the respective examination: For MSc (02 years) Students: SSC/equivalent: 05% FSc/equivalent: 05% BSc/equivalent (02 years): 10% MSc/equivalent (02 years): 10% MS/M.Phil/ equivalent: 30% GAT Subject/ GRE International (Subject): 40% Interview/ Presentation Approval of Departmental Board of Graduate Studies (DBGS) For BSc/BS/BE (04 years) Students: SSC/equivalent: 05% FSc/equivalent: 05% BSc/BS/BE/ equivalent (04 years): 20% MS/M.Phil/ equivalent: 30% GAT Subject/ GRE International (Subject): 40% Interview/ Presentation Approval of Departmental Board of Graduate Studies (DBGS)
--- Notes: ---
Only those MS & PhD candidates who have passed the BS/ MS/ M.Phil./ equivalent examination shall be eligible for admission to the next higher class. The result of pre-requisite degree/certificate for admission in any discipline should be officially notified by the respective body/ authority of the university till the date of closing of admission. It is mandatory for MS/ PhD applicants to pass the GAT/ GRE/ HAT in the relevant field of study with required marks/ score to apply for admission in IST. However, provisional admission without GAT/ GRE/ HAT may be granted only for first semester subject to availability of seats. Applicants with a foreign qualification are required to obtain the equivalent certificate from HEC before applying for admission in MS/ PhD programs offered by IST. It is mandatory for Government employees to submit NOC from their organization on registration day If a sufficient number of students are available only then Program/ Specialization will be started.

[TOPIC: Fee Refund Policy]
Fee Refund Policy

The date of request for fee refund claims will be considered from the date of receipt of application through email at refund@ist.edu.pk To apply for refund, an applicant is required to email refund request at refund@ist.edu.pk along-with the reason of leaving IST, scan copy of paid challan form/s & copy of CNIC of Guardian Application Processing Fee and Admission Fee are non-refundable. From Registration, all students are required to complete NDC to process the refund of dues (if any) as per fee refund policy Tuition Fee, Sports Fee, Library Fee and Laboratory Fee, service charges, Safe/Smart Campus Charges, Endowment Fund, Library Development Fund are refundable within 15 days of convene of classes on a pro rata basis as given below. No refund shall be admissible after 15 days of convene of classes either one joins IST/avail facilities or not.
Table 1:
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: Up to 7th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 100%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: From 8th – 15th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 50%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: From 16th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 0%
If admission is offered after commencement of classes, date of commencement of classes will be considered as mentioned in offer letter. Adjustment of student liability from 2nd Semester onward will be based on class attendance & facility will be charged as per actual. The application for the adjustment of student’s liability will only be entertained on the verification by concerned HoD and approval of Registrar. In case of Admission cancellation in 1st semester cheque of fee refund will be issued in the name of student’s father/Guardian. 100% dues will be refunded in case where student could not attain requisite marks/grade for admission as prescribed by the institute. This policy is only applicable on fresh admissions. However Optional Charges will be deducted as per actual usage of facilities based on 4.5 Month/Semester Basis. In case of semester freeze, fee once deposited will be adjusted in next semester as per freeze policy after re-joining the student. If student wants to leave the institute after freezing his semester, then dues will be refunded according to refund policy present at the time of freezing the semester. Transport Charges and Hostel Charges (Dormitory Charges, Shuttle Service Charges, Washing Charges) are refundable within 30 days of registration on a pro rata basis as given be

[TOPIC: Vision]
Vision

To establish a world class facility for conducting root cause failure analysis and provision of materials processing advisory to industry, thereby acting as catalyst for enhancing safety in operations & improving quality standards in design and manufacturing.

---

[TOPIC: Mission]
Mission

Provisioning of affordable materials-related solution to our valued customers by addressing their specific needs.

---

[TOPIC: Philosophy]
Philosophy

NCFA believes in delighting its valued customers all the way by offering one window, customized, flexible, cost effective, comprehensive, wide ranging and authentic solutions as per their specific needs pertaining to issues/matters related to metallurgy, especially in the field of Failure Analyses. NCFA expects that our high quality and timely delivered services shall be instrumental in establishing long term relations with our valued customers thereby entailing a win-win situation for all. NCFA fully realizes the sensitivity of its services and their long term legal and safety implications; by default we consider ourselves accountable for all our actions and find under moral obligation to perform all agreed tasks (within contractual terms & conditions) with integrity & secrecy while maintaining impartiality.

---

[TOPIC: Services]
Services

--- Root Cause Failure Analysis (RCFA) ---
This is a systematic process of identifying the factors that caused a failure and the actions that can be taken to prevent or mitigate future failures. RCFA can help you improve the reliability and performance of your equipment, reduce maintenance costs, and enhance safety.
--- Replica Metallography ---
This is a non-destructive technique of obtaining the microstructure of a metal or alloy without damaging the original specimen. Replica metallography can be used to evaluate the condition of in-service components, such as welds, heat-affected zones, coatings, and corrosion products.
--- Corrosion Damage Assessment ---
This is a comprehensive evaluation of the extent and severity of corrosion damage on a material or structure. Corrosion damage assessment can help you determine the root cause of corrosion, the remaining life of the component, the risk of failure, and the best remedial actions.
--- Reverse Engineering ---
This is a process of analyzing the design and functionality of a product or system by taking it apart and examining its components. Reverse engineering can help you understand how something works, improve its performance, or create a new product based on an existing one.
--- Remaining Life Assessment (RLA) ---
This is a method of estimating the remaining useful life of a component or system based on its current condition, operating history, and expected future conditions. RLA can help you optimize the maintenance and replacement schedules, extend the service life, and reduce the risk of failure.
--- Fatigue Life Estimation ---
This is a technique of predicting the number of cycles or hours that a component or system can withstand before it fails due to fatigue. Fatigue life estimation can help you design and select materials that can resist fatigue, evaluate the effect of loading and environmental conditions, and prevent premature failures.
--- Fitness for Service (FFS) ---
This is an assessment of the ability of a component or system to continue to operate safely and reliably in its current or intended service. FFS can help you evaluate the impact of defects, damage, or degradation on the structural integrity, functionality, and performance of the component or system.
--- Creep Damage Assessment ---
This is an evaluation of the degree and distribution of creep damage on a material or structure. Creep damage assessment can help you identify the factors that influence creep, measure the cree

---

[TOPIC: The Team]
The Team

Dr Anjum Tauqir Director Dr Anjum Tauqir did his masters in metallurgical engineering from Moscow Institute of Steel and Alloys and PhD from University of Connecticut. He has more than 30 years of experience in energy beams-materials interaction, development of hi-purity aerospace alloys and failure analysis. He has his credit more than 80 publications in reputed journals, international conferences and two patents registered in UK. He has established multi-billion semi industrial hi-tech projects in the field of metallurgy-taking them from planning to production. Presently he is professor at Materials Science and Engineering Department, Institute of Materials Science, Islamabad and as Advisor in NCFA. Contact: +92 051 907 5678 Email: ncfa@ist.edu.pk
Dr Syed Wilayat Hussain Advisor Dr Syed Wilayat Husain did his BS in Metallurgical Engineering from Dawood College, Karachi. He got his MS and PhD in Metallurgy from the University of Connecticut, USA. He has more than 30 years of experience in Alloy Development, Materials Characterization and Failure Analysis. He has 50 plus international publications to his credit and has remained associated with projects of national importance. Presently he is a Professor in the Department of Materials Science & Engineering at IST. He is currently also employed as Advisor in NCFA.
Engr Obaid Ur Rauf Project Lead Engr Obaid Ur Rauf did his Bachelors and Masters degree in Materials Science & Engineering from Institute of Space Technology (IST), Islamabad and has specialized in Metals & Alloys. His research interests includes Failure Analysis of Aerospace Materials and Advanced Composite Materials, Root Cause Failure Analysis, Non-Destructive Testing and On-site/ Replica Metallography. Currently he is working as Project Lead in NCFA. Contact: +92 051 907 5734 Email: ncfa@ist.edu.pk
Engr Waseem Arshad Project Lead Engr Waseem Arshad got his Masters in Mechanical Engineering from SMME NUST and Bachelors in Materials Engineering from SCME NUST. He has been involved in various industrial projects. His key areas of interest are Root Cause Failure Analysis, Remaining Life Assessment (as per API 579), In-situ/ Replica Metallography, Metal and Alloys, Manufacturing, Non-destructive Testing, Engine Tribology and Reverse Engineering. Currently he is working as Project Lead in NCFA. Contact: +92 051 907 5734 Email: ncfa@ist.edu.pk
Engr Umer Rasheed Graduate Trainee Engineer – GTE Engr Umer Rasheed serves as a Graduate Trainee En

[TOPIC: Introduction]
Introduction

The Department of Student Affairs at the Institute of Space Technology aims to foster holistic development and a sense of community among students. Our objectives include: Nurturing well-rounded personalities through co-curricular and extracurricular activities Creating an inclusive environment that promotes a sense of belonging Promote teamwork, social responsibility, and ethics in our students Providing platforms for diverse students to grow personally and professionally Encouraging student engagement and alumni connections locally, nationally, and globally
Introduction

---

[TOPIC: Quality Policy]
Quality Policy

Department of Student Affairs is committed to serve IST students, by addressing their needs related to co-curricular and extracurricular activities. We aim at constantly reviewing our service provision procedures to ensure that the department lives up to the student expectations.
Quality Policy

[TOPIC: Message by Head]
Message by Head

The Department of Student Affairs empower IST students by providing them a range of experiences to make their on-campus stay valued and memorable. We believe in upholding a balance of academic and co-curricular engagements that is vital for developing students into well-rounded personalities and thus equip them with skills to fulfill their professional aspirations. We are committed to broaden students’ horizons and to inspire a sense of belonging with their alma mater. Department of Student Affairs strives to foster insights of teamwork, leadership, civic engagement and morality in students by providing various avenues for their personal and professional development. Our efforts are inclined towards effective student engagement so that we accomplish the important task of strengthening the society with an influx of responsible citizens. Raza Butt Head/ Additional Director Department of Student Affairs
Message by Head

[TOPIC: Student Committees]
Student Committees

Student involvement is the driving force behind effective administration and campus management at the Institute of Space Technology (IST). Through active participation in student committees, students contribute valuable perspectives that shape policies and services, enhancing campus life. This collaborative approach enables IST to address student needs and maintain a well-functioning campus environment. Our student committees span various domains: Sports Facilities and Gymnasium/Fitness Center Food Services (Mess, Tuck Shop, etc.) Campus Management (Building/Block-Wise) Hostels Management (Hostel-Wise)
Student Committees

---

[TOPIC: Internships]
Internships

With our students, being recognized for their technical acumen and professional excellence, Internship Office Works closely with Government & Private Organizations to secure internships for IST students to enhance their technical capabilities as well as providing them with much needed professional exposure. As part of their degree requirements, students must complete an internship of approximately 6–8 weeks during their studies. It does not only enable them to familiarize themselves with the latest techniques and developments in the field but also provide real word use of their academic learnings. Organisations Interested in providing internship opportunities to our students can reach out at studentaffairsist@gmail.com
Internships

---

[TOPIC: Placements]
Placements

IST graduates are consistently recognized for their strong technical abilities and managerial potential, making them highly sought after across public and private sectors. Office of Internship, Placement & Alumni Affairs at IST facilitate meaningful industry engagement and support successful career transitions of its graduates. Job Fair, an annual event, serves as a dynamic platform connecting students with top employers while highlighting the university’s academic and research strengths. The fair enables student-industry interactions including on-campus interviews of students from our graduating class. Central to this initiative is the Graduates Book, a professionally curated publication featuring detailed student profiles and project abstracts. It is shared with participating employers to provide a clear snapshot of the talent emerging from IST. Annual Job Fair also offers a chance to enhance IST’s institutional brand by fostering collaboration, demonstrating its talent pipeline, and reinforcing its role as a hub of innovation and excellence. Organisations Interested in providing Placement opportunities to our students can reach out at studentaffairsist@gmail.com

---

[TOPIC: Sports Facilities]
Sports Facilities

IST has provided students with the facilities of indoor and outdoor games. Indoor facilities including fully equipped physical fitness gym, Snooker, Carom, Chess and Table Tennis. Outdoor facilities available to students are Cricket and Football grounds, Lawn Tennis, Volleyball, Basketball and Futsal Court. The students are encouraged to play in their spare time.
Sports Facilities

[TOPIC: Inter-Departmental Sports]
Inter-Departmental Sports

Sports play a great role in life of a student as it keeps students healthy and active. Being involved in sports activities benefits students in many ways. It not only provides physical strength; it also increases their mental abilities. Playing together as team develops a sense of team spirit and facilitate in building strong relationship with each other. IST holds sports competitions among its various departments. In addition to the interdepartmental sports activities, students have ample opportunities for routine sports activities during their free time between classes and in the afternoon.

[TOPIC: Champion Department]
Champion Department

The sports events are held on league basis at IST. The winning teams of the pool matches compete in the final for the title. The winner team is awarded with the Departmental Trophy. The best players (Male/Female) of the respective sports events are awarded Gold Medals during the Annual Award Ceremony in which parents of the winning students are also invited. The Champion Department for year 2023-24 is Department of Electrical Engineering.
Champion Department

[TOPIC: Co-Curricular Societies & Clubs]
Co-Curricular Societies & Clubs

American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) - IST Chapter AIAA is a promising platform that provide incentives for students to undertake hands-on work in the aerospace field. AIAA-IST chapter collaborates with relevant industries to support students in research findings at the national and international level. Organizing seminars and workshops with other aerospace related institutions and offers students the opportunity to join international associations, allowing students to connect with professionals in the field around the world. American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) – IST Chapter ASME and is a global association that promotes the dissemination of education, knowledge and the respective skills required for the research and development for the discipline of engineering. This society is focused on mechanical engineering. ASME has over 110,000 members in more than 150 countries worldwide. As a society different competitions and workshops are hosted so that the students may acquire skills outside the classroom. Institution of Mechanical Engineers (IMechE) – IST Chapter Institution of Mechanical Engineers (IMechE) is a global association that promotes mechanical engineering and its applications in different industries. IMechE has over 120,000 members in as much as 140 countries worldwide. The student chapter of IMECHE at IST strives to provide students with opportunities to get guidance from top professionals in the relevant industries. Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) - IST Chapter IEEE is a non-profit and multi-disciplinary organization with 10 regional setups. Pakistan is part of region 10 and IST affiliates with IEEE Islamabad section. The main objective of this IEEE – IST Chapter is to facilitate and motivate the students of IST towards technology that will enhance their technical and professional skills in the field of Electrical and Electronic Engineering. Material Advantage Chapter - IST (MAC) This society provides an opportunity for the young learners to discover their skills and explore new horizons in Materials Science and Engineering. In order to incorporate these qualities in the students, the society arranges multiple activities through its club such as materials research, seminars, workshops etc. American Society of Heating, Refrigeration and Air-Conditioning Engineers (ASHRAE) – IST Chapter ASHRAE advances the arts and sciences of heating, ventilation, air condit
