[TOPIC: Center for Vibration Testing and Condition Monitoring]
Center for Vibration Testing and Condition Monitoring

Center for Vibration Testing and Condition Monitoring

---

[TOPIC: Vision and Mission]
Vision and Mission

--- Vision ---
The Center for Vibration Testing and Condition Monitoring (CVCM) is envisioned as leading academic and research facility that will provide technical solutions to wide variety of audience.
--- Mission ---
The CVCM is committed to promote research and provide technical support in the emergent field of vibration and condition monitoring to our scholars, customers and public and private sector organizations for improving the quality of the products.
--- Objectives ---
To provide the services of vibration testing and condition monitoring against critical installations. To identify the key issues like unbalance, misalignment, bent shaft, rolling bearing faults, eccentricity, resonance, looseness, rotor rub, fluid-film bearing instabilities, gear faults, belt/sheave problems etc. To provide training towards clients need with substantial exposure to industrial and real world problems.
Vision and Mission

---

[TOPIC: Our Team]
Our Team

Dr. Asif Israr presently working as Professor and Head Mechanical Engineering, has vast involvement in the field of vibrations and condition monitoring for the last 21 years. He has worked on several industrial and engineering projects involving structural dynamics, vibration analysis, fracture mechanics and structural health monitoring.
Dr. Muhammad Zubair Khan has over 35 years of diversified practical experience of working on various R&D; projects and problems. He has hands-on experience to overcome the vibration problems in rotating machinery parts.
Dr. Muhammad Umer has more than 7 years of research, teaching and industrial experience in a number of national as well as international Institutes. He has worked in collaboration with world famous multinational organization GE (General Electric Aviation) and Ansaldo Energia Italy to investigate the vibration mitigation of the turbine blades. His area of research and specialization is nonlinear vibration dynamics and numerical modeling for the finite element analysis.
Ruqia Ikram is working as Lecturer in Department of Mechanical Engineering. She worked on vibration related projects. Her research areas include nonlinear vibration, structural design and analysis.

[TOPIC: Advanced Manufacturing Facility]
Advanced Manufacturing Facility

Advanced Manufacturing Facility

---

[TOPIC: Vision and Mission]
Vision and Mission

--- Vision ---
To lead academic research and commercial operations in the area of Advanced and Precision Manufacturing.
--- Mission Statement ---
To nurture a culture of R & D among academia, public and private sector organizations and industries by providing solutions and consultancy in Precision Manufacturing. Objectives To provide exemplary service in automated machining, welding, CAD/CAM, and manufacturing in general. To optimize manufacturability of engineering products through robust fixture design, time-saving toolpath creation, advanced CAM techniques, and financially viable tooling selection. To impart technical knowledge via professional training and design consultancy for promotion of research and industrial endeavors.
Vision and Mission

His research interests include advanced manufacturing technologies, micromachining, sustainable production as well as optimization strategies to minimize production costs.

[TOPIC: Our Team]
Our Team

--- Avionics System Design Lab ---
Irfan Majid, PE Irfan Majid holds a post graduate avionics degree in Avionics Engineering & presently is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Avionics Engineering. He is a seasoned Avionics Engineer with over thirty years’ experience. His work experience includes avionics maintenance & testing, life cycle support, R&D work for avionics systems of manned and unmanned aircraft. He is Senior Member of IEEE and nominated on Avionics System Panel of IEEE Aerospace Electronics System Society. The panel promotes avionics related activities worldwide and provides guidance for R&D work. His current research areas include certification for AI/ML in safety critical avionics systems, Communications Navigation & Surveillance (CNS) systems architectures for Urban Air Mobility and Aircraft Electromagnetic Compatibility analysis. He has authored a number of research publications and a book chapter. He regularly offers tutorials in Digital Avionics System Conference.
Dr. Suhail Akhtar Dr. Suhail Akhtar, Fellow Royal Aeronautical Society, received his B.Eng. degree from NED University, Karachi, Pakistan. He holds a DEA in Control Systems from Université de Technologie de Compiègne, France and MS and PhD Degrees in Aerospace Engineering from the University of Michigan, USA. His research interests include non-linear dynamics state estimation and control for aerospace applications, aircraft dynamics at high angles of, adaptive disturbance rejection and H_8 attitude estimation and control on SO(3).
Dr. Israr Hussain Dr. Israr Hussain holds a Doctorate degree from University of Manchester, UK. Currently HoD of Department of Avionics Engineering. He is an experienced Avionics engineer with over thirty years of experience. His research areas are Image Processing and synthetic vision. He has actively worked in implementation of image processing algorithms on FPGAs. Since 2002 he has held various teaching and management positions in academic institutions.
Dr. Muhammad Abdul Rehman Khan Dr. Muhammad Abdul Rehman Khan is a Professor in the Department of Avionics Engineering. He is a highly experienced Avionics Engineer with almost forty years’ experience. His experience with avionics systems encompasses maintenance, life cycle support, integration and design & development. His MS & PhD is in the field of Communication systems and has worked in design of Microwave and Millimeter wave links. His doctorate thesis was related to system c

[TOPIC: Research Areas]
Research Areas

Flight Control Systems With the ever increasing demand for greater degrees of agility and autonomy of aerospace vehicles, has led to major strides in flight control algorithms and system architectures. There laboratory maintains strong multidisciplinary systems orientation that focuses on linear and nonlinear systems, dynamic optimization, feedback control, optimal path planning and decision-making in uncertain environments as well as computational and software aspects of flight systems. The current graduate research in the lab is focused satellite attitude control as well as flight planning and flight control of dynamically unstable aircraft using H-infinity control methods on SO(3) and lie groups in general along with AI enabled real time trajectory generation for complex flight maneuvers involving post stall flight regimes. For undergraduate students the lab supports and complements the theory courses in flight dynamics & controls, with actuated simulation platforms and experiments to emphasize implementation issues, as well as case studies of specific aerospace vehicles. Autonomous Guidance and Navigation Systems A surging interest in space launch operations and in Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) concepts is exacerbating the limitations of current practices, still heavily reliant on airspace segregation and not supporting the multimodal/intermodal evolution of air and space transport. For a successful integration of these new transport modes, it is critical that an acceptable level of safety is provided, requiring the development of novel digital tools (e.g., mission planning and decision support systems) that utilize advanced Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies to allow a seamless integration of space operations in the current ATM network. The research work explores the role of Aerospace CPS (ACPS) and AI research to enable the safe, efficient and sustainable development of the air and space transport sector in the next decade. While the technical maturity of propulsive and vehicle technologies is relatively high, there are several opportunities and challenges associated with the adoption of CPS and AI to enable the integration of point-to-point suborbital spaceflight with conventional atmospheric air transport. Current research aims at developing robust and fault-tolerant CPS architectures that ensure trusted autonomous air/space transport operations with the given hardware constraints, despite the u

[TOPIC: Electro-Magnetic Compatibility (EMC) Solutions]
Electro-Magnetic Compatibility (EMC) Solutions

Electro-Magnetic Compatibility (EMC) Solutions

---

[TOPIC: About]
About

--- Where Excellence meets Compliance ---
Electro-Magnetic Compatibility (EMC) is the branch of Electrical Engineering concerned with unintentional generation, propagation and reception of Electro-Magnetic Energy which may cause unwanted effects such as Electro-Magnetic Interference (EMI) or even physical damage in Operational Equipment. An exponential growth has been witnessed in the Professional Prowess of Pakistani Engineers in a multitude of spheres during recent couple of decades. Owing to a number of higher education scholarships, we now have numerous engineers with doctorate degrees. Contributions in research work have flourished and engineering development has grown manifold. However, one sphere where skills are lacking is the transformation of conceptual designs and laboratory models into market ready products. This attribute is largely dependent upon practical experience and cannot be attained through academic knowledge alone. One key component in this aspect is the Electro-Magnetic Compatibility (EMC) Compliance. The EMC compliance is a mandatory requirement for all Electronics / Electro-Mechanical products in Military and Commercial spheres, not just to fulfill regulatory obligations but to ensure that the product performs as envisaged in all possible Electro-Magnetic Environment conditions. We offer efficient, broad range EMC Solutions by highly experienced professionals by Identifying potential EMC problems early in the design process and stage-by-stage depiction of products potential EMC-related design flaws. This helps in avoiding inconvenient and costly product redesigns at the final phase of the design plan. Owing to the experience and knowledge base available with Department of Avionics Engineering, a venture has been launched with an endeavor to make EMC related testing and skills available nationwide. In line with the core engineering domain of the department, Aerospace Electromagnetic analysis, test plans and Electro-Magnetic Interference mitigation is a distinctive feature of the facility.

---

[TOPIC: About Us]
About Us

The Center for Surface Engineering and Tribology (CSET) operates at the intersection of research and industry. We provide cost-effective, sustainable, and reliable solutions in surface engineering, coatings, and tribology to extend component life and improve system performance.
--- Mission & Vision ---
--- Mission ---
Deliver high-quality, innovative, and eco-conscious engineering solutions that empower industries to achieve optimal performance and sustainability.
--- Vision ---
Lead nationally and regionally in surface engineering and tribology—recognized for technical excellence, industrial impact, and sustainable practices.

---

[TOPIC: Services & Capabilities]
Services & Capabilities

At the Center for Surface Engineering and Tribology (CSET), we provide a comprehensive range of solutions that address industrial challenges in wear, corrosion, performance, and efficiency. Each service is delivered with technical precision, industry insight, and a commitment to cost-effectiveness.
--- Industrial Coating Solutions ---
We specialize in advanced coating technologies that extend component lifespan, reduce maintenance downtime, and improve operational reliability. Capabilities include: Plasma Spray Coating: High-quality surface protection for critical parts. High-Velocity Oxy-Fuel (HVOF): Dense, durable coatings with superior wear and corrosion resistance. Cold Spray Coating: Low-temperature application ideal for repairing sensitive components without thermal damage.
--- Tribology & Wear Testing ---
Our tribology laboratory delivers accurate measurement and analysis of friction, wear, and lubrication to help industries enhance material performance and prevent premature failures. Services include: Tribometry and wear behavior analysis Friction coefficient measurement Lubricant performance testing
Services & Capabilities

---

[TOPIC: Industries that we served]
Industries that we served

We work with a wide range of industries where performance, reliability, and sustainability are mission critical.
--- Aerospace ---
High-performance coatings and materials solutions for aircraft components to improve wear resistance, reduce weight, and enhance operational safety.
--- Marine ---
Anti-corrosion and protective coatings for ships, offshore structures, and deep-sea equipment, ensuring long-term performance in harsh environments.
--- Automotive ---
Wear-resistant coatings and component testing for high-performance vehicles, improving efficiency and extending service intervals.
--- Oil & Gas ---
Corrosion protection and coating solutions for pipelines, valves, and machinery operating in extreme conditions.
--- Power Generation ---
Coatings for turbines, boilers, and rotating equipment to maximize efficiency and minimize downtime.
--- Strategic Organizations ---
Customized materials engineering solutions for defense, aerospace, and critical infrastructure projects with uncompromising reliability.
Industries that we served

---

[TOPIC: Our Applications]
Our Applications

--- Where our solutions make a real-world impact ---
Our technologies and expertise find real-world applications across industries where performance, reliability, and safety are non-negotiable.
--- Medical Implants ---
Biocompatible coatings and surface treatments to improve implant longevity and performance.
--- Gas Turbine Parts ---
High-temperature, wear-resistant coatings to extend service life in power generation and aerospace turbines.
--- Automotive Components ---
Surface engineering for critical engine, transmission, and braking system parts.
--- Deep Sea Applications ---
Anti-corrosive solutions for subsea structures and marine exploration equipment.
--- Electrically Conductive Parts ---
Specialty coatings to enhance conductivity while preventing oxidation.
--- Petrochemical Industry Equipment ---
Coatings that resist chemical attack and reduce downtime.
Our Applications

[TOPIC: Director QEC's Message]
Director QEC's Message

Quality in higher education remains a global challenge. As technological advancements continue to evolve, higher education institutions play an important role in nurturing a knowledge-based economy and providing individuals with skills across various disciplines. Quality Enhancement Cell (QEC) at IST is committed to improving the quality of education, research, governance, and compliance with the standards set by the regulatory bodies. Since its inception, QEC has been actively involved in various initiatives, such as performing program reviews and evaluations of institutional performance, conducting and analyzing surveys, reviewing KPIs, ISO certification, and organizing seminars and workshops centered on quality assurance. Faculty, students, and staff are encouraged to participate in efforts aimed at enhancing academic quality and achieving institutional excellence. Hence, QEC at IST is dedicated to the continuous improvement of all academic and administrative functions and fostering a quality culture through collaboration with faculty, students, and staff. With the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) across all sectors, higher education institutions now face a major challenge in upholding the high quality of teaching and research. It is, therefore, essential to encourage individuals at IST to use AI responsibly and critically to elevate the quality standards in education. I believe that under the visionary leadership of the Vice Chancellor and the professional dedication of QEC and IST officials, we will successfully achieve all milestones in accordance with national standards. Prof. Dr. Asif Israr Director QEC
Director QEC's Message

[TOPIC: Business Incubation Center(BIC)]
Business Incubation Center(BIC)

Business incubation center
Business Incubation Center(BIC)

---

[TOPIC: About Us]
About Us

Business incubation center at IST is established to strengthen the successful growth of economy and social development through supporting technology based startup. We are providing an entrepreneurial environment and guiding our startup to develop essential skills they need to launch a successful venture. Our main goal is to churn out financially viable and freestanding startups. We aspire to develop individual talent, skills and personality to give each business the best possible start, thus fostering entrepreneurship at a local and national level. Vision To be the center of innovation, creativity and entrepreneurship for the budding entrepreneurs from IST, local and national community to make their dreams a reality Mission To be the Business Development Service Providers for the startup founders from IST (Faculty, students and alumni) and passionate entrepreneurs from the local and national level. BIC Team Our integrated team is engaged in optimizing the productivity of innovative startups and enabling them to operate efficiently in the competitive market.

---

[TOPIC: Aim]
Aim

BIC at IST aims to facilitate new company formation by providing an entrepreneurial nurturing environment for young start-up and spin-out technology companies; alleviating their survival and growth, thus encouraging them to reach their full potential. We aspire to develop individual talent, skills and personality to give each business the best possible start, thus fostering entrepreneurship at a local and national level.

---

[TOPIC: Objective]
Objective

To foster an entrepreneurial culture, by providing the students, alumni, and faculty, opportunities to transform their business ideas to reality. These facilities are also open to the general public, provided they can either leverage IST’s knowledge base or contribute to IST’s knowledge base. To facilitate the availability of IST resources to the incubatees in a mutually beneficial way. To provide a conducive working environment to the incubatees to nurture their innovative ideas. To liaise with private and public sector funding sources, government agencies, industrial associations, chambers of commerce and industries to provide facilitation and networking for incubate companies.

---

[TOPIC: Services]
Services

--- Admin and Physical Support: ---
Incubatees will be provided furnished offices with following facilities: Internet access Dedicated telephone line. Centralized fax, photocopy, printing and mailing via courier. Conference room facilities equipped with multi-media Display of company’s profile on IST/BIC website Email address on IST/BIC domain. Security, safety, maintenance, housekeeping, janitorial services Canteen services for tea, coffee, meals, and soft drinks. Common reception services
--- Business and Professional Support Services ---
Access to IST library and digital resources. Networking opportunities within IST as well as industry and government resources. Support in marketing through website and use of IST/BIC name. Provide guidance and mentorship through professionals and specialized consultants. Identification of various funding options for incubates. Advisory services in business plans formulation, marketing strategies, project selection etc. Training on basics of entrepreneurship. Advice in legal and financial consultancy.
--- Technical & Management Services: ---
Access to Faculty resources Access to Labs Access to Venture Capital Firms

[TOPIC: Pre-Incubation Program]
Pre-Incubation Program

In recent years, the world has observed a revolutionary shift from job seekers to job creators. Business Incubation Center at IST working with the aim to encourage entrepreneurial culture and mindset among IST students, faculty, alumni and general public. After having many interventions for IST students, BIC has launched a Pre-Incubation Program to encourage and facilitate IST students to create their own Technology based startup to become the job creators rather a job seeker. Furthermore, the program focus would be to provide an opportunity to IST students to commercialize their innovative ideas. Selected startups under Pre-incubation program would have access to all support and facilitation of IST Business Incubation Center and a desk space in a Co-working space dedicated for Pre-Incubation at IST BIC. BIC at IST provide range of facilities for IST student: Internet access Co-working office space Utility services Access to lab and equipment Access to funds Legal and business consultancy Well educated workforce Advance computer labs Our mentors and business consultants are those who are devoted and dedicated to accelerate the growth of startups. They are Encouraging IST students to think innovatively and enable them to perform efficiently in such a competitive market. Registration: 
Pre-Incubation Program

[TOPIC: About KICSIT]
About KICSIT

Director's Message
--- Engr. Masood Khalid Director Incharge KICSIT ---
Its my pleasure to welcome you at Dr. A. Q Khan Institute of Computer Sciences and Information Technology (KICSIT), Kahuta, a campus of Institute of Space Technology (IST), Islamabad. It has the privilege that it was inaugurated in November 2000 by Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan himself, the founder and then Chairman of KRL. KICSIT aims to offer quality education at an affordable cost. Currently, it is offering two four-year degree programs as Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering (BSCE) and Bachelor of Science in Computer Science (BSCS). BSCE is accredited by PEC (Pakistan Engineering Council) and BSCS is accredited by NCEAC (National Computing Education Accreditation Council). KICSIT is ideally located in an exclusively peaceful and secure environment. The Institute does have an elite faculty with foreign-qualified PhDs and MS degree-holders having decades of practical industrial experience, KICSIT is housed in its own purposefully-built air-conditioned campus. In view of its increasing students strength and scope, the New Campus of KICSIT has been developed next to the lush green and scenic hills of Kahuta. This is on main Rawalpindi-Kahuta Road and covers an area of above 100 Kanals. Though KICSIT is a small-size institute, yet by grace of Almighty it has a shining record in terms of students and alumni. A large number of alumni have joined Strategic Organizations, Armed Forces and Multinational Companies. In higher education, many studied and got degrees from US, UK and other Foreign Universities. Also, KICSIT students have been competing well in the inter-university competitions and have won many awards in sofrware, hardware and extra carrucullar areas, showing a nice grooming level prevalent here. Thus, overall it is expected that our students find their stay at KICSIT a memorable and fruitful time period of their lives. For detailed academic and administrative information, please visit the official KICSIT website.
Visit KICSIT Official Website
About KICSIT

[TOPIC: Admissions]
Admissions

Introduction The admissions for BSCS are held twice a year in Spring and Fall Semesters. The admissions for BSCE are held once a year in Fall Semester. The maximum number of seats available for admissions in BSCS is 100 per year, BSCE program is 40 per year. The admissions for MSCS are held twice a year in Spring and Fall Semesters. FEE SUBMISSION You can pay fee in any branch of Askari Commercial Bank or by easypaisa with following details. KICSIT Account No 01761650500136 Account Title: KICSIT -KRL Kahuta Application Processing Fee Rs.2000/-
Apply Online

---

[TOPIC: Financial Aid & Scholarships]
Financial Aid & Scholarships

Scholarships for BSCS and BSCE Every year KICSIT provides scholarships exceeding worth Rs. 2.0 Millions to the talented and needy students. The number of scholarships KICSIT provides, exceeds the percentage requirement of scholarships set by HEC. . Merit Award for Students (1st To 8th Semesters) Merit Award is given on the basis of excellent academic performance to Students from 1st To 8th Semesters. The top three position-holders in each semester are given this award worth 50%, 37.5%, & 25% of tuition fee (of KRL Wards) waivers respectively. To qualify for this award students should have earned SGPA of 3.0 or above for semester II to VII and 70% marks or above in Intermediate result for first semester. In case of equal SGPAs, positions are given on the basis of higher percentage. This award is not dependent on any other scholarship. Need Based Scholarships KICSIT provides financial support to talented needy students in the form KICSIT Need Based Scholarships. This scholarship is open for all onboard students and they can be from any fee category. The applicant should have obtained SGPA of 3.00 or above (70% or above in FSc for first year students).The applicant should submit application form along with all relevant documents. at the start of the semester after final result notification from KICSIT CoE office. Scholarships will be awarded for only one semester but can be re-availed in subsequent semesters, if the need prevails and for this student will have to put up fresh application. A student can avail only one scholarship at a time except Merit Award. Decision for the award of scholarships and amounts will be made on case to case basis as recommended by Scholarship Committee and approved by Director General KICSIT. HEC Ehsas Undergraduate Scholarship The Ehsas Undergraduate Scholarship Project focuses on the most critical segment, namely the 4-5year undergraduate programs, which contributes the most to income of the individual who completes the bachelor’s degree. It is one of the sizeable project funding under Government of Pakistan's Ehsaas social safety net program which aims to provide 50,000 undergraduate scholarships with an average budget of Rs.6 billion annually. Over a period of next four to five years these scholarships will be increased to around 200,000. Scholarship covers university tuition fee and stipend throughout four-five years of degree duration. It is a significant step to ensure affordable higher educat

[TOPIC: News and Activities]
News and Activities

--- IRS Team at TAKRA’26: Showcasing Innovation in RC Aircraft and Avionics ---
We're thrilled to share that our talented IRS team proudly represented our institution at TAKRA'26, held from 12-15 February at University of Central Punjab (UCP). This prestigious event brought together top teams from across the country, providing a platform for students to demonstrate their skills in Remotely Controlled Aircraft Design, Avionics Integration, and Flight Operations. Our team's exceptional performance at TAKRA'26 is a testament to the outstanding quality of our Avionics program, which offers students hands-on experience in: - Aircraft Design and Development - Avionics Systems and Integration - Flight Operations and Testing - Teamwork and Problem-Solving Despite facing tough competition and technical challenges, our students demonstrated remarkable determination, teamwork, and dedication, showcasing their ability to apply theoretical concepts to real-world problems. At IST, we're committed to providing our students with opportunities to push the boundaries of innovation and excel in their chosen field. The experience gained by our students at TAKRA'26 will undoubtedly benefit them throughout their academic and professional careers.
--- Avionics Department in Collaboration with IST Robotics Society Conducted  a Successful 2-Day RC Aircraft Design Workshop ---
Avionics Department, in collaboration with IRS Society, successfully conducted a 2-Day RC Aircraft Design Workshop at Westminster Academy, Islamabad on 16–17 February. The workshop provided students with hands-on exposure to RC aircraft design fundamentals, aerodynamics concepts, and practical building techniques. Participants actively engaged in learning, designing, and understanding the real-world applications of avionics engineering. It was an exciting experience to inspire young minds towards innovation, technology, and aviation.
News and Activities

TOPIC  : Research Section

Research Section

Research,
Innovation and Discovery
Research Excellence
The IST Office of Research, Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) fosters collaborative environments among researchers, industrial partners, national R&D organizations, and funding agencies. Since its inception in 2011, ORIC has been facilitating value-added innovation in both basic and applied research, developing promising innovative processes, products, and technologies according to market-driven requirements. Our research quality reflects the highest international standards, advancing IST's stature among the world's best research institutions.
IST Lunar CubeSat ICube-Q

PAGE   : Student Affairs & Campus Life
TOPIC  : Campus Life Section

Campus Life Section

Student Experience
Campus Life
Building a vibrant community of creative and accomplished people from around the world
Facilities at IST
Vibrant student life fostering growth and leadership
Overview Going to IST is about making the most of your life as a student. As you get involved, you'll not only have a lot of fun, you'll also develop your leadership abilities, raise your consciousness, and acquire skills you'll use after graduation. Together students, faculty and staff of IST create a welcoming and enterprising community to achieve all this. List of student societies is always evolving with the changing interests of students. Are you interested in academics, athletics, socializing, social work, performing arts, gaming or movies? There's a good chance that a student society exists for what you want to do. Wellness and Counseling Center Wellness and Counseling Center, IST is dedicated to help students, faculty and staff members maximize their sense of overall well-being and facilitate them towards making healthy contributions to their academic, personal, social and career development. The Centre provides free and confidential on- campus psychological counseling services, both in individual or group settings, where the students address their concerns with a highly trained mental health clinician in a safe and non-judgmental environment. The Centre further aims to highlight social stigma and spread awareness regarding various topics of mental health through workshops, advocacy and outreach programs. For your convenience, there are many ways to book a counseling appointment for your professional and personal concerns with either a male or a female therapist, You can email our psychologists directly at annum.tanweer@ist.edu.pk or taj.iqbal@ist.edu.pk You can call directly at 051-9075859 between 9 a.m to 12 p.m - Monday to Friday. You can visit Department of Humanities and Sciences, IST or Wellness and Counseling Center at Second Floor, Rashid Block, IST. Housing & Dining Hostel Registration Form Boarding Facility Boarding facilities will be limited to students living outside Rawalpindi / Islamabad area. IST is managing 03 x hostels for boys and 1 x hostel for girls. Students are expected to bring decent colour linen and their amenities with them at the time of joining. Boarding facility will only be given depending on availability of rooms. Cafeteria Services The cafeteria services in IST are provioded by Cafeteria Contractor on cash basis. A spcious and well managed cafeteria is available on campus. However, cafeteria services in outside hostels are provided by Cafeteria Contractor(s) and Owner of Girls Hostel for girls, on fixed monthly basis. Tuck Shop Services A well-organized Tuck Shop is available on campus which caters students’ needs and serves all kind of beverages and snacks etc. Dining Timings Dining timings at IST and outside hostels are as under:- Working Days Breakfast - 0700 hrs to 0800 hrs Lunch - 1130 hrs to 1500 hrs Dinner - 2000 hrs to 2230 hrs Weekends / Holidays Breakfast - 0730 hrs to 1000 hrs Lunch - 1330 hrs to 1430 hrs Dinner - 2030 hrs to 2230 hrs Quality Assurance Committees A committee headed by a senior Administration member, Representatives of faculty, administration and students is constituted to check / cater the standard / quality of Cafeteria Services on campus and outside hostels. Laundry Facility Laundry facility is available at IST campus which caters the washing and ironing requirements of all boarding students. Fixed charges of laundry shop are paid by students on semester basis. Attendant Services One attendant in each wing of the hostel would be available for cleaning of rooms and other minor chores. The charges of the attendant will be born by students themselves. Health & Safety IST Safety Department is responsible for developing and maintaining a safe and secure environment for the employees and students so that they can pursue their academic, professional and personal ambitions. Committed to serving with professional excellence, we strive to be approachable, reliable and truly service-oriented in the performance of our duties. The department maintains strong, professional relationships with all stake holders to ensure continuous collaboration on emergency planning efforts and familiarity with our campus in the event of an emergency. The following facilities are available at IST for Safety of individuals and premises during emergency. Fire Safety Facilities All buildings of IST have dedicated Emergency Exits and are also equipped with fire extinguishers, Water Hydrants. In addition, some areas e.g. data center are equipped with latest firefighting system (FM-200), smoke and heat detectors. IST also has an in-house, highly trained firefighting team that is equipped with latest Fire Tenders and other modern firefighting equipment. Health Facilities Health care is an important and necessary part of any organization. The true mark of an organization is how it looks after its employees and others. Institute of Space Technology provides medical cover to its employees and students. Medical cover consists of Professional man-power, MI Room, First Aid Facility, Ambulance for 24 hours and First Aid boxes at different locations. The employees of Institute and their families are entitled to free medical treatment from panel hospitals. IST is providing necessary Information, Instructions and training regarding Health, Safety and Environment to all at IST Campus.

Convocation 2025
A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements!
A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements! The atmosphere was electric today as we congratulated the brilliant graduates of the 18th Convocation of IST . Your hard work, dedication, and perseverance have finally culminated in this magnificent success. Today, we didn't just award degrees; we launched future leaders, innovators, and change-makers. The entire IST family is immensely proud of each and every one of you. Go forth and conquer! The world awaits your brilliance.
Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics"
Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics.” The session went beyond theoretical discussion, focusing on how language subtly shapes everyday experiences. It highlighted the ways in which word choices, silences, and patterns of speech reflect underlying structures of power, identity, and gender often in ways that remain unnoticed. The event was particularly enriched by the active engagement of the participants. Their insightful questions, personal reflections, and willingness to reconsider familiar linguistic practices through new perspectives made the session especially meaningful. Such interactions reaffirm Dr. Ausima’s continued commitment to research, teaching, and critical dialogue in the field of sociolinguistics.
Faculty Participation in the Pakistan English Conference - 2025
Ms. Qurat-ul-Ain and Ms. Ifrah Jamil , lecturers Humanities and Sciences Department, attended the Pakistan English Conference titled “Rethinking English Studies in the Global South”, at Government College University, Lahore on November 27, 2025. During the conference, Ms. Qurat-ul-Ain and Ms. Ifrah Jamil presented their research paper titled “Teaching Undergraduate Students: A Methodological Shift from Pedagogical to Andragogical Approach” and highlighted the evolving needs of undergraduate learners and emphasized the importance of adopting andragogical strategies to enhance student engagement and learning outcomes. Their participation contributed to the ongoing academic dialogue on the future of English studies in the region and provided valuable representation for the Institute of Space Technology at a national academic forum
2025 IAF Wrold Space Award given to Chang'E6 Lunar Mission including ICUBE-Q
Every year IAF (International Astronautical Federation) gives two World Space Awards....
Every year IAF (International Astronautical Federation) gives two World Space Awards. One in individual category and one in team category. In 2023 Elon Musk got the award in individual category and James Webb Space Telescope team (from NASA CSA and ESA) team category. This year 2025 IAF Space Award in team category has been awarded to Chang'E-6 Lunar team that includes all Payloads (including our ICUBE-Q). Award ceremony will be held during annual IAC in Sydney Australia.

Engineering Beyond the Classroom
The International Conference on Engineering Advancements, Science and Technology (ICEAST)
The aim of the International Conference on Engineering Advancements, Science and Technology is to....
The aim of the International Conference on Engineering Advancements, Science and Technology is to foster interdisciplinary collaboration among engineers, scientists, researchers, and industry professionals, providing a global platform for presenting cutting-edge research and technological innovations. Click on link to see details.
The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST)
The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST) marks a....
The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST) marks a transformative milestone in Pakistan's journey through space science and technology. As the largest space event in the country, ICAST unites the legacies of IST’s International Conference on Aerospace Science and Engineering (ICASE) and SUPARCO’s International Conference on Space (ICS) into a biennial global forum of unparalleled excellence. ICAST bringing together visionaries, global thought leaders, pioneering researchers, and industry trailblazers, aspires to redefine the future of space science by fostering collaboration, presenting transformative research, and exploring interdisciplinary space applications with the potential to reshape our collective future.

Vision & Mission

--- Vision ---
Be a National torchbearer, in the realm of academics, through quality teaching, robust research and outreach – to produce leaders in the field of Space Science and Technology, in line with National aspirations.
--- Mission ---
To provide a conducive environment for realization of the full potential of faculty and students. To provide a conducive environment for realization of the full potential of faculty and students. To produce competent, balanced and creative graduates capable of assuming leadership positions to tackle future national/international technological challenges. To create awareness of and interest in Space Education at all societal tiers for sustainable national development.
Vision & Mission

PAGE   : About IST
TOPIC  : Harassment Complaint Cell

Harassment Complaint Cell

The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is committed to fostering an all-inclusive and supportive learning environment for its community. In abidance with The Higher Education Commission’s (HEC) Policy on Protection against Sexual Harassment in Higher Education Institutions , IST has a zero-tolerance policy for any untoward conduct that violates the dignity of any individual within the institution. The Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC) was established on 20 February 2012, for monitoring and protection against sexual harassment of any individual at IST. It has since developed policies and procedures to address sexual and gender-based harassment so that members of the IST community feel safe and secure in a harassment- free environment since they have an avenue to voice their grievances. For further information about the policies or harassment issues, kindly contact: Dr. Rahila Naz, Head of the Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC), IST Room number 212, Block 2, IST 051-9075562 head.hcc@ist.edu.pk Every member of the IST community has the right to work and conduct learning, research, and scholarship without being subject to any form of discrimination, harassment, or social pressure. The HCC’s investigative process is designed to be confidential and fair. No reprimand will be initiated for filing a legitimate complaint. HCC will ensure the protection of the rights of the complainant and the accused. Eliminating harassment from educational institutions requires a cohesive effort by its community – the HCC is IST’s spearhead in this endeavor.
--- Harassment Policy ---
Document: HEC Policy Guidelines on Harassment (2023-07-19)
Document: Protection Against Harassment of Women at the Workplace Act, 2010 (2023-06-19)
Harassment Complaint Cell

PAGE   : About IST
TOPIC  : Visiting IST

Visiting IST

Away from congestion, noise and pollution of the city, at approx. 20 minutes drive from Islamabad and Rawalpindi, having multiple access through Islamabad Highway and GT Road, IST is located in the Capital Territory of Pakistan. This advantageous location offers round the clock accessibility through public and private transportation.
--- From Rawalpindi/Islamabad through Faizabad ---
From Faizabad it is about 13 Mins, 19.7 KM drive Through Faizabad Interchange , enter into Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) and continue driving towards Islamabad Airport Continue onto Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) pass through Khanna Bridge ( about 4.6 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) pass through Koral Flyover (Old Air Port Chok) and enter onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) ( about 3.06 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) pass through Kaak Pul (Suan River Bridge) ( about 8.1 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) the road will slightly curve right and you will find Canyon Views-EMAAR ( on the left 2.2 KM ), keep in right lane for the U-turn ahead Immediately after Canyon Views-EMAAR take the U-turn just before the Islamabad Toll Plaza( about 250 meters ) After the U-turn take the first left turn out from Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) ( about 300 meters ) Stop for security check and then turn right, Parking is straight ahead and IST is on the Left
--- From Saddar, Rawalpindi, through Katchehri Chowk ---
From Katchehri Chowk it is about 15.2 km, 15 mins drive From Katchehri Chowk , continue onto G.T. Road towards Rawat Drive for about 7.9 KMs and take a Slight right onto Sawan River Bridge and continue onto G.T. Road After about 5.8 KMs Turn left onto Islamabad Highway Pass through Islamabad Toll Plaza ( about 360 meters ) After toll plaza take the first left turn out from Islamabad Highway ( about 380 meters ) Stop for security check and then turn right, Parking is straight ahead and IST is on the Left
Visiting IST

PAGE   : Programs & Admissions
TOPIC  : Why IST

Why IST

Space is considered to be the final frontier and world’s best minds take up the most daunting challenges to conquer it. Fazeel Mahmood Khan, PhD The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is a premier institution in Pakistan, renowned for its focus on space and related sciences. Here's why IST stands out: Why Choose IST? 1. Specialized Education: IST offers specialized education in space and related sciences, equipping students to address national and global issues. 2. Research Opportunities: The institute has state-of-the-art research labs, providing students with hands-on experience and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research. 3. Accreditation and Recognition: IST is federally chartered, HEC recognized, and PEC accredited, ensuring its degrees are valued globally. 4. Industry Connections: IST has strong industry connections, facilitating internships, placements, and job opportunities for its graduates. 5. Student Life: The institute offers a vibrant student life, with various societies, clubs, and sports activities, fostering personal growth and development. 6. Scholarships and Sponsorships: IST offers merit-based scholarships and sponsorships, making quality education accessible to deserving students. 7. Global Recognition: IST's programs are accredited under Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), substantially equivalent to Washington Accord, enabling graduates to work globally. Unique Achievements 1. Satellite Development: IST has successfully developed and launched satellites, including iCUBE1 and ICUBE-Qamar, demonstrating its expertise in space technology. 2. Research and Innovation: The institute is committed to research and innovation, with a strong focus on solving real-world problems. Career Opportunities At the IST, we empower students to pursue their passion for space science and technology. Since 2002, we've been producing professionals who meet the demands of national organizations and contribute to the nation's technological advancements. Thriving Careers Ahead Our graduates enjoy excellent career prospects, both locally and globally. They're employed in leading organizations, including: • Space agencies (SUPARCO, European Space Agencies) • Aviation industry (Airbus, Emirates, Air Blue) • Research institutes (R&D, academia) • Government organizations (PTA, WAPDA, EPA) • Private sector (mobile communication, agriculture, airlines) Diverse Employment Opportunities Our multidisciplinary approach opens doors to various industries, contributing to socioeconomic activities nationwide. Our graduates work in: • Space science and technology • Aerospace engineering • Research and development • Telecommunications • Meteorology • Agriculture and food sector Global Recognition IST graduates are recognized globally, with opportunities to pursue further education at renowned universities worldwide. If you're passionate about space science and technology, IST is the perfect platform to launch your career. With our strong industry connections, research opportunities, and global recognition, you'll be well-equipped to succeed in your chosen field.

PAGE   : Programs & Admissions
TOPIC  : Admission FAQ

Admission FAQ

Q: What is the application process?
A: The application process involves filling out an online application form, uploading required documents, and paying the application processing fee. Online admission module can be accessed by clicking the link IST Admissions
Q: How many seats are for each program?
A: Engineering Programs: Aerospace Engineering : 100 - Avionics Engineering : 80 – Electrical Engineering : 100 – Mechanical Engineering : 120 – Metallurgy & Materials Engineering : 80 Computing Programs: Computer Science : 100 – Artificial Intelligence : 50 – Data Science : 50 – Software Engineering: 50 – Computer Engineering: 50 Science Programs : Space Science : 50 – Physics: 40 – Math: 50 – RS&GIS: 50 – Chemistry (Nanotechnology): 50 – Biotechnology: 50
Q: What is duration of study for each Program?
A: The study duration of each program is 04 years (08 semesters).
Q: How many times admissions are announced for Engineering, Non-Engineering & Computing Programs in a year?
A: Admissions for BS programs are announced once in a year i.e. in March/ April.
Q: Does an applicant can apply for all engineering, computing & science programs?
A: Yes.
Q: What is the eligibility criteria, merit determination criteria & fee structure for BS programs?
A: Updated information is available and can be accessed by using the below links: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=general-eligibility&-scholarship= https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=merit-determination-criteria&-scholarship= https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=fee-structure-bs-programs&-scholarship=
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS engineering programs?
A: IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. Please use the below link for details: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=entry-test&-scholarship=
Q: I have already appeared for entry test but I do not receive result card. What should I do?
A: Please get a print of your result card from website of testing authority and upload with online application form
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS computing & non-engineering programs?
Q: Does the institute have affiliation with any university/ institution?
A: IST is a federally chartered, HEC recognized and PEC accredited, degree awarding university therefore it does not require affiliation with any other university/ institution. Please use the below link for details: https://www.hec.gov.pk/english/universities/pages/recognised.aspx
Q: Does Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) recognize the Institute?
A: IST has been awarded accreditation for its various undergraduate engineering programs at Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), making them fully compliant with the Washington Accord. Engineering graduates obtaining a degree under this category of HEIs are considered at par with world class professionals in the field, and do not require any further evaluation of their degrees for professional jobs or higher education abroad.
Q: Does National Computing Education Accreditation Council (NCEAC) recognize the Institute?
A: All Computing programs offered by the institute are duly approved/ accredited by the National Computing Education Accreditation Council (NCEAC).
Q: What is the medium of teaching/ instructions/ examination/ extracurricular activities at IST?
A: All educational and extracurricular activities such as “Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc” at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language. Therefore, IST graduates may not face any difficulty in performing job/ pursuing higher education at any institution/ university/ organization around the globe where the medium of communication/ teaching/ instructions/ examination is English.
Q: Does IST have reserved seats for overseas/ dual nationality holder applicants?
A: There are no reserve seats for overseas/ dual nationality holder applicants. All admissions in IST are based on transparent open merit basis.
Q: Does IST have reserved seats for DAE applicants?
A: There are no reserved seats for Diploma of Associate Engineering (DAE) applicants. However, DAE holders can apply on open merit, competing with FSc Pre-Engineering and ICS students.
Q: Does IST allow result awaiting DAE applicants to apply for admission in IST?
A: Result awaiting DAE applicants are not eligible to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Does IST allow pre-medical applicants to apply for admission in BS engineering & science programs?
A: Those pre-medical candidates are allowed to apply for admission in BS engineering & science programs who have already passed additional math papers or appeared for additional math papers and waiting for result announcement. OR by completing 8-week condensed Mathematics course from any university.
Q: Does IST allow pre-medical students to apply for admission in BS computing programs?
A: Yes, pre-medical students can apply for all computing programs.
Q: Does the institute provide hostel facility?
A: Yes, IST offers furnished hostel facilities for both boys and girls within the campus, providing a convenient and secure living environment for students.
Q: What is the lowest merit of last years for BS programs?
A: Lowest merit for each program has remained different every year. Therefore, apply online irrespective of lowest merits of last years.
Q: What are the Sponsorships/ Scholarships are offered by IST for BS students?
A: Details about the Sponsorships/ Schlarships can be accessed by using the below link: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=financial-aid-and-scholarships&-scholarship=
Q: Does IST have enough qualified faculty?
A: Yes, IST has enough highly qualified and experienced faculty in the respective areas.
Q: Does IST have proper lab facilities?
A: IST is proud to maintain well-equipped laboratories that fully support our academic programs. Additionally, we have established specialized laboratories to equip students with technical proficiency and expertise in practical equipment handling, thereby ensuring their readiness for real-world challenges
Q: Does IST have a library?
A: Yes, IST has a resourceful library with sufficient current editions of books, reference material to support the programs, which is constantly updated. Library is also subscribing more than 14 periodicals and online professional publications.
Q: What are the prospects of higher education?
A: The IST programs are accredited nationally and internationally and the student shall have no difficulty in getting admissions abroad.
Q: What is the System of Education at IST?
A: IST follows the semester system. Each program is of eight semesters and each semester is of eighteen weeks duration. There will be two semesters in each year.
Q: Is it mandatory for students of O-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC before the closing date to apply for admission in IST?
A: Yes, it is a mandatory requirement for students of O-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC and upload with online application form before the closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Is it mandatory for students of A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST?
A: Yes, it is mandatory requirement if an applicant have passed/ completed A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification. In other case, such students are required to apply for equivalence certificate immediately after the completion/ announcement of results of A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification from concerned boards.
Q: How students can apply for equivalence certificate in current environment?
A: IBCC has introduced an online system to apply for equivalence certificate. Email address/ contact numbers are also mentioned on IBCC website to obtain desired/ relevant information. Please visit IBCC website https://www.ibcc.edu.pk and apply online for equivalence certificates of O-Level/ A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification.
Q: How much time is given to new BS students for the submission of final FSC result card/ equivalent certificate if it is not available at the time of submission of online admission form?
A: It is mandatory for all BS students to submit the final FSC result card/ IBCC equivalent certificate on registration day. In case of delay in the announcement of results from concerned board, students are required to submit the final FSC result card/ IBCC equivalent certificate within two weeks after the announcements of results from concerned boards otherwise their admission will be cancelled.
Q: What if student of FSC/ A-Level/ equivalent level foreign qualification comes to know after the announcement of result that he/ she could not meet the eligibility requirements of IST?
A: Admission of such students will be cancelled. It is the responsibility of such students to immediately inform Admissions Office so that their admission can be cancelled and full fee can be refunded. Disciplinary action can be initiated in case of delay or hiding the result for longer time from IST. In such case, fee will be refunded as per IST fee refund policy.
Q: Is it mandatory to deposit application processing fee?
A: Yes, it is mandatory to deposit application processing fee in any online branch of HBL otherwise your application will not be processed/considered for admission in IST. Application processing fee is non-refundable in any case.
Q: How it will be confirmed that whether my application processing fee has been received at IST? Or I have to send copy of Paid Chillan form to IST.
A: Nothing is required to send IST. Candidates are required to provide payment details and upload challan forms/ transaction slip with online admission form. Your fee status will be automatically updated on your application form within next 4 to 5 working days after deposit of application processing fee in any online branch of HBL. You can log-in and check the status.
Q: Can I deposit application processing fee through online bank transfer?
A: It is strongly recommended to deposit your application processing fee through bank challan available at online admission form.
Q: What should I do if I have paid application processing but my fee status is not updated on online application form?
A: You are required to email scan copy of paid challan form at “admissions@ist.edu.pk” before the closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Is it mandatory to attach the documents with online application form?
A: Yes, it is mandatory to attach the desired documents with your online application form otherwise your application will not be processed/considered for admission in IST.
Q: I have lost one of the documents which are required to attach with online application form. What should I do?
A: Apply for duplicate copy from concerned board. Meanwhile you can download your result card from website of concerned board and attached with your online application form.
Q: I have lost my FSC (Part-1) result card but I have FSC (part-1 + part 2 combined) final result card. What should I do?
A: Please upload FSC final result card with your online application form.
Q: What I have to mention in admission form if my guardian don’t have (National Tax Number) NTN?
A: Mention NTN number of your guardian, if available, otherwise leave that cell empty.
Q: What does it mean by Guardian?
A: A guardian is a person who is looking after you in terms of taking your care, paying your educational expenses etc.
Q: My aggregate score in 1st year is less than 60%. Can I apply for admission in IST?
A: If you are sure that you will be able to improve your score after FSC, as per eligibility criteria mentioned on IST website for respective program, then you may apply for admission in IST based on your score in FSC (Part-1).
Q: I have completed FSC/ ICS. Can I mention the overall score of FSC or my merit will be determined on the basis of overall FSC/ ICS marks?
A: You are required to mention the score of FSC/ ICS (Part-1) where required because as per merit determination criteria, merit will be determined based on the marks obtained in FSC/ ICS (Part-1).
Q: When my application will be processed?
A: If you have deposited application processing fee then please wait. Your application will be processed soon.
Q: When and where merit lists are displayed?
A: Merit lists are posted on IST website within one week after the closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Is it required to send hard copy of online application form/ Challan Form or documents to IST?
A: You are required to upload desired documents with your online admission form. Do not send application form or any other document to IST.
Q: What is the last date to deposit application processing fee?
A: Last date to deposit application processing fee is same as closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: What have to mention by O-Level or A-Level students on online application form where Reference Number is required?
A: Reference Number is mentioned just below the line where obtained marks by the student are mentioned on equivalence certificate.
Q: What have to mention by O-Level or A-Level students on online application form where Serial Number is required?
A: Serial Number is given on top right corner of equivalence certificate.
Q: What have to mention by FSC students on online application form where Registration Number is required?
A: FSC students are required to mention registration number issued by concerned board.
Q: Can I edit information in my online application form after submission?
A: Yes, you can edit/update your online application form up to closing date to apply for admission in IST by using your log-in details. In the same way, you can also upload or replace existing documents which are attached with your online application form. When you will upload new document, the previous one will be deleted automatically.
Q: Can I access my online admission form on mobile?
A: Yes, you can access/ edit your online admission form through mobile. However, for better experience, please use desktop computer to access/ edit the information in your online admission form.
Q: I have error in my email address or CNIC number. How I can rectify it?
A: You are required to send an email at “admissions@ist.edu.pk” highlighting the problem which you are facing while creating login or accessing your online application form.
Q: I have completed my FSc (pre-medical) and appearing/appeared for additional math paper. Can I apply for admission to Engineering programs?
A: Yes, you can apply for admission to Engineering programs in IST based on your score in FSc pre-medical (part-1).
Note:

The university reserves the right to make any corrections or changes, as and when required, without prior notice.
Admission FAQ

PAGE   : Programs & Admissions
TOPIC  : Foreign Applicant

Foreign Applicant

--- Al Khwarizmi Scholarships for Foreign Students ---
Scholarships are available for Foreign Students to study in Bachelor/ Master/ Doctoral Degree Programs offered by IST. Duration of Scholarship: For BS (Bachelor’s) Students: Upto 04 years For MS (Master’s) Students: Upto 2.5 years For PhD (Doctoral) Students: Upto 04 years The scholarship program covers funds for the following expenses: University tuition fee as per actual Stipend @ PKR 15000, 20000 & 35000 per month for students admitted in BS, MS & PhD programs respectively Stipend @ PKR 50000 per month for scientist/ post doc fellow One time return air ticket Mandatory academic & non-academic charges like admission fee, endowment fund, library development fund, service charges, safe/ smart campus charges, hostel charges etc. are not covered.
--- Admission of Foreign Applicants in IST ---
--- Admission Pre-requisites ---
Candidates willing to apply for admission in BS, MS/ PhD programs offered by IST must: meet the eligibility requirements ( BS , MS , PhD ) for respective program as mentioned on IST website be able/ willing to pay the semester dues/ allied charges as per fee structure ( BS , MS, PhD ) given on IST website for foreign Students. All fees and other charges are subject to revision from time to time be proficient in English as all educational and extracurricular activities such as "Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc" at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC / HEC , Pakistan (keeping in view the eligibility requirements to apply for respective program) before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST. In case any document proves to be false, fake, or fabricated at a later stage, a provisionally admitted student shall be liable to expulsion from the University at any stage or to any other disciplinary or legal action the University may deem necessary obtain No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their Ministry of Foreign Affairs or their Embassy in Pakistan prepare personal statement, in English, should include self-introduction, study and work experience, reasons for application and study plan/ research proposal obtain two letters of recommendation from full professors or associate professors, in English. (Professors’ signature, contact phone number and email address must be put on the letter) be willing to meet/ abide all requirements/ rules & regulations issued by the IST/ HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations/ Departments from time to time possess adequate mental and physical health to continue his/ her course of studies
--- Procedure ---
Admissions will be announced by IST in national newspapers/ on IST website Foreign applicants meeting the pre-requisites will be required to apply online at IST’s website www.ist.edu.pk Foreign applicants will be required to complete online application form in all respects including the deposit/ online transfer of application processing fee before the last date to apply for admission in IST for further process Merit of foreign applicants will be determined according the merit determination criteria mentioned on IST website Merit lists of provisionally selected BS and MS candidates shall be displayed on IST website whereas, provisionally selected PhD candidates will be intimated through email Provisionally selected BS, MS & PhD candidates will deposit admission dues as per details shared by IST to secure their seat Cases of selected foreign applicants for provisional admission in IST will be referred to Higher Education Commission (HEC) and relevant Government department(s) along with their passport copy, photographs, admission letter, NoC of their Embassy/ Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Student’s Information Sheet for Issuance of No Objection Certificate (NoC)/ final approval as per the policy in vogue Selected foreign applicants for provisional admission in IST will be required to approach the Pakistani mission abroad for the visa and submits the following documents: Application Form (Student Visa Form) Photocopy of Passport Photograph Educational Documents Admission Letter of University No Objection Certificate (NOC) issued by the Home Country to Study in Pakistan Any other document if required by Pakistan Embassy at respective country Joining instructions will be issued to the provisionally selected foreign applicants after: The completion of all admission requirements of IST Receiving the intimation/ NOC from HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations Successfully joined foreign applicants/ students will be required to meet/ abide all requirements/ rules & regulations issued by the IST/ HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations/ Departments from time to time IST reserves the right to make amendments in the policy regarding the admission of foreign students at any time without notice/ cancel or refuse admission to any foreign applicant at any time/ stage without assigning any reason
--- Contact Details ---
Incharge Admissions Focal Person (International Student Office) Email: admissions@ist.edu.pk Contact: +92-51-9075100

TOPIC  : Financial Aid & Scholarships

Financial Aid & Scholarships

--- Sponsorship for BS Students ---
Several elite National Research & Development (R&D) organizations provide sponsorships to the deserving BS students of selected programs after test/interview. The sponsorship covers tuition fee and a guaranteed job after graduation, provided all requirements of the sponsoring organization are fulfilled by the candidate.
--- Merit Scholarships for BS Students ---
IST offers merit scholarships to three students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per the existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of admissions merit and semester results. Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to BS students of each batch of each undergraduate program offered by IST:-
Table 1:
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: Ser. | col_1: Scholarship Type | col_2: Merit Position | col_3: Tuition Fee Waiver | col_4: Minimum Criteria
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 01 | col_1: IST-1 | col_2: 1st | col_3: 100 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 02 | col_1: IST-2 | col_2: 2nd | col_3: 50 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 03 | col_1: IST-3 | col_2: 3rd | col_3: 25 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: FOR SUBSEQUENT SEMESTERS:
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: Ser. | col_1: Scholarship Type | col_2: Merit Position | col_3: Tuition Fee Waiver | col_4: Minimum Criteria
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 01 | col_1: IST-1 | col_2: 1st | col_3: 100 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 02 | col_1: IST-2 | col_2: 2nd | col_3: 50 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 03 | col_1: IST-3 | col_2: 3rd | col_3: 25 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
GENERAL CONDITIONS: Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship. Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. However, both SGPA and CGPA will be considered to ascertain the eligibility of the student for Scholarship. Students getting any type of scholarships/ sponsored by any organization are not eligible for merit scholarship. Student will be awarded only one scholarship (merit or need based) from IST’s own funds (whichever is higher) at a time. In case of two or more students securing the same merit position (Admissions’ Merit Position/ same SGPA and CGPA), allocated amounts of respective scholarships for these positions shall be equally distributed among them. If students ‘A’ & ‘B’ jointly declared at 1st position, then the student ‘C’ would be at 3rd position and so on. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is an ‘I’ Grade in preceding semester. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is a ‘W’ Grade in preceding semester. Student should take full load of credit hours in that preceding semester. In case, a student takes any additional/ repeat course(s), his/ her merit position will be calculated on the basis of normal load of credit hours in that semester. In case of semester freeze, merit scholarship would only be awarded after re-joining of the student. Transferred students shall not be eligible for the award of merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they studied with the students of previous department/ program. However, such students shall be reconsidered for merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they will study with the students of their new department/ program. In situations where existing rules & regulations are found silent/unavailable, the decision made by IST will be the final which can’t be challenged anywhere. The contents as stated in merit scholarship policy are expression of intent only. The institute reserves the right to discontinue any portion or make amendments in merit scholarship policy/ rules & regulations at any time without notice.
--- Merit Scholarships for MS Students ---
IST offers merit scholarships to two students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of semester results. Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to students of each batch of each MS program offered by IST:-
Table 1:
Ser: 1 | Scholarship_Type: IST-1 | Merit_Position: 1st | Tuition_Fee_Waiver: 100% | Minimum_Criteria: SGPA and CGPA > 3.75
Ser: 2 | Scholarship_Type: IST-2 | Merit_Position: 2nd | Tuition_Fee_Waiver: 50% | Minimum_Criteria: SGPA and CGPA > 3.50
GENERAL CONDITIONS: Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship. Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. However, both SGPA and CGPA will be considered to ascertain the eligibility of the student for Scholarship. Students getting any type of scholarships/ sponsored by any organization are not eligible for merit scholarship. Student will be awarded only one scholarship (merit or need based) from IST’s own funds (whichever is higher) at a time. In case of two or more students securing the same merit position (Admissions’ Merit Position/ same SGPA and CGPA), allocated amounts of respective scholarships for these positions shall be equally distributed among them. If students ‘A’ & ‘B’ jointly declared at 1st position, then the student ‘C’ would be at 3rd position and so on. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is an ‘I’ Grade in preceding semester. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is a ‘W’ Grade in preceding semester. Student should take full load i.e. minimum 09 credit hours in each 1st & 2nd semester and minimum 06 credit hours in each 3rd & 4th semester. In case, a student takes any additional/ repeat course(s), his/ her merit position will be calculated on the basis of normal load of credit hours in that semester. In case of semester freeze, merit scholarship would only be awarded after re-joining of the student. Transferred students shall not be eligible for the award of merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they studied with the students of previous department/ program. However, such students shall be reconsidered for merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they will study with the students of their new department/ program. In situations where existing rules & regulations are found silent/unavailable, the decision made by IST will be the final which can’t be challenged anywhere. The contents as stated in merit scholarship policy are expression of intent only. The institute reserves the right to discontinue any portion or make amendments in merit scholarship policy/ rules & regulations at any time without notice.
--- EHSAAS Scholarships for BS Students ---
Higher Education Commission (HEC) with the support of Government of Pakistan has launched “EHSAAS Undergraduate Scholarship Project” for the qualified students from low income families to complete undergraduate studies (Four-Five-year Bachelor programs) from public sector Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) of Pakistan, Azad Jammu & Kashmir, and Gilgit Baltistan. The principle objective of the EHSAAS Undergraduate Project is to provide assistance to the maximum number of students who have secured admission to the universities in various disciplines but lack of financial means to continue their studies. The needy students admitted in IST are informed to apply for EHSAAS Scholarship as and when announced by HEC.
--- HEC Need Based Scholarships for BS Students ---
Pakistan has no shortage of talented youth, but in several instances, lack of resources prevents them from realizing their potentials. This becomes more problematic when it comes to accessing higher education. The Government of Pakistan acknowledges these constraints and has tasked Higher Education Commission to facilitate the needy and deserving students through Need Based Scholarships. Scholarships are announced for financially disadvantaged students of as many as 94 public sector universities and their campuses. The needy students admitted in IST are informed to apply for HEC Need Based Scholarship after the completion of admission process subject to availability of funds/seats.
--- The Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) ---
The Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) under guidance of the Board of Directors is providing opportunities to the talented but needy students to fulfill their dreams and aspirations and at the same time to become partner in the development and welfare of Pakistan. The PEEF, since its inception in 2009 has awarded more than 374,000 scholarships to students of Punjab and other provinces which are currently pursuing education through public and private sector institutions. Needy students, admitted in IST, can also apply for this scholarship. The details are available at https://www.peef.org.pk/Scholarships_Main.html
--- Loans ---
Interest free loans are also provided to IST students by National Bank of Pakistan (NBP). The process to apply for loan and other details are available at https://www.nbp.com.pk/studentloan/
